text: I think this is a great Starbucks location in Tampa. It's located on the corner of a busy intersection, at Kennedy and Westshore, making it equally accessible for you to leave and go take care of multiple things that you need to do in the area or, for you to come from various locations in the area to take a quick coffee break. Unlike Starbucks locations that are closer to downtown, this location is located in a plaza with ample parking, making it a more stress free option when you are on your coffee run. The inside of the store is spacious, with lots of table seating. I also really like the addition of high top table seating in the middle of the store. It makes for a more communal feel and it also allows slightly larger groups of people to congregate and meet up, always a plus for a coffee shop. The staff here are courteous and take care of your drink or food orders fast. And the bathrooms are quite clean, another plus considering the high volume of people that pass through the store. Will definitely drop by if I'm in the area again :)

text: It's Starbucks so by now unless you live under a rock you should know what you're going to get. So with that said, the x factor is the employees, the restaurant space itself and the cleanliness of the restaurant.   The employees at this location have always been kind in my multiple visits here. Today I received a coffee and the barista asked if I could wait for her to pour a new batch because the coffee I received was on the stale side. That goes a long way because just the other week down the street at another location, I did receive a stale Starbucks coffee and it's pretty disappointing. That's your pick me up at work that you've been craving for the last two hours and then you finally get it and realize you should have just had the regular stuff in your work's cafeteria. So I'm appreciative that this staff goes the extra mile and acknowledges your presence when you enter.   As far as the space goes, it's not a large space but this location is never crazy busy so I've never seen a seating issue here so it appears to be just right. And cleanliness has also never been an issue here, there's never packets of sugar thrown around the condiments counter or trashed tables.  Overall, it's a Starbucks location that has good employees, always seems to have seats available and is clean. Definitely recommend if you like Starbucks.

text: Today is supposed to be my day for returning phone calls and emails, and lots of paperwork. But horror of horrors, I've been on the road so much that I completely forgot to check the status of the pantry for green tea before I started boiling the water this morning. Fearing a bad case of "the shakes" from early morning tea withdrawal, I grabbed my car keys and headed down the road to Starbucks.  This particular Starbucks sits on the corner of Dale Mabry and Linebaugh Avenue within walking distance of my condo. When I first moved to Carrollwood the space was occupied by a skanky Long John Silvers which thankfully closed, was gutted by the developer, and re-opened as a really nice Starbucks. The interior looks less like an industrial-strength coffee house, and more like a very comfortable study / reading room with lots of places to sit and relax.  I'm not a big fan of coffee, and especially not the jumbo grande mocha frappacino double-shot espresso salted caramel latte crap that most people seem to like. Instead I just wanted a basic cup of freshly-brewed green tea -- oh, and one of those yummy-looking blueberry scones as well, please.  The barrista seemed to appreciate my needs -- perhaps it was the dark circles under my eyes, and the way I was chewing on my lower lip -- and she quickly rushed to pour me a large green tea and fork over the scone.  *sighs happily*  The tea was flavorful and calming, and the scone was fresh and delicious. I found a corner table and sat down to read the front section of the Tampa Bay Times while watching the other harried morning commuters lining up for their fresh Shot o'Joe before starting their work day.  This particular Starbucks opens at 5 in the morning, and stays open till 11 or so at night. They have a drive-through window which seems popular with the vapid "latte crowd" who are too busy blabbing away on their cellphones to come into the store -- fine by me, like......ya know?  Well done Starbucks, and thanks for saving my day!

text: Love, love, LOVE my Starbucks - it's not secret.  This location offers up all the standard Starbucks fare but it's a tad smaller than most and the parking is limited :-(  You can only get into it from the St. A Trail or Bellerose Drive on one side of each road because it's in a high traffic area.  Consequently, it can take a little extra time compared to some of the more readily accessible locations.  Be sure to download the Starbucks App to your smart phone in order to earn stars for every purchase (Buy 12 get 1 free) and offers and discounts that are offered exclusively to the App holders such as an extra hour on Frappy 50% off days, B1G1 free from time to time, or the more frequently offered $1 off an espresso beverage.    You can also put money on your app, link it to a gift card, or register for auto load with a credit card so that you can conveniently pay on your phone!  One tip if you're trying to get to Gold member status or earn more stars, request to pay for your purchases separately so that you can earn a reward star for each transaction.  Another thing is remember you can customize the drinks anyway you like.  For example, you can get a Frappuccino made your way - it's doesn't have to be made with the sugary base.  You can ask for it to be made with decaf espresso or made with dark roast coffee, milk of your choice, ice and a sugar free syrup if you want to go sugar free.   You can google all kinds of Starbucks off shelf or secret menu ideas/recipes for your next visit!  And if you're doing a LCHF diet, remember that you can always ask for your lattes or fraps to be made with half and half, heavy cream or whipping cream!  If you order any of their drinks as a "Breve" they will make it with half and half rather than milk.    The point is, customize the drinks to what YOU want!

text: No no no no I was thrilled to see this place open near my home.  My first trip 7am Sunday morning and the bathrooms have lock codes on them. I asked the guy behind the counter why and was told so they can monitor who is using the bathroom. I hate this kind of snobbery so I won't be back.   I'm guessing this is an anti-homeless measure but there are no lock codes at the Grand and Lindell location or the S Kingshighway location and homeless people frequent those areas without over running the coffee shops. Locking the doors helps no one but it reveals something sad about the owners heart.  This is a brand new store so the owner isn't reacting to a historical situation. Perhaps they are acting on faulty assumptions about this part of town.  Maybe they are worried about losing business Whatever, doesnt matter. As said earlier, I won't go back. There are plenty of other Bucks in STL and there's a perfectly wonderful privately owned coffee place closer to my house anyway -Restituto (without locked bathrooms). There are two other local coffee shops in a 3 block radius and they don't have locked bathrooms or hordes of "undesirables" hanging around.  I love you Starbucks but this anti-human snobbery breaks my heart. What if a random homeless person happens by and really needs a bathroom break? I know what that is like because I'm one of the millions of Americans who has been in that situation. text: No line in drive through and super sweet employees.  After my son's 2 1/2 year old wellness check, I told my friend we'd stop at Starbucks on our way to see her. This was on our way home, so it was a quick stop. Usually I go to the Starbucks on Racetrack, because it is a tad bit closer, but I'm starting to question my decision. That one always seems to have a line of cars (but it does move super quickly). At 10:40 a.m. on a Thursday, there were no cars at the drive through! The entire time I was there I didn't see another car. That's bananas to me. I'll have to come back and see if this holds true.   I was thrown for a loop when I tried to order a pumpkin cream cold brew and a chocolate chip cookie. They didn't have either. The pumpkin cream cold brew is seasonal, which makes me sad. The employee was seriously one of the most helpful people I've ever met. She kept asking me about my taste preferences and offering suggestions. With other drive throughs, the employee probably would have just given me time to look over the menu and make another decision, but she seemed invested in helping me choose something I would like.  I ended up getting chocolate covered madeleines (for my 2 1/2 year old) and an Irish cream cold brew for myself. Both were huge hits. The Irish cream cold brew was the perfect amount of sweetness for me while still packing a caffeine punch and my son ate all the chocolate off the madeleine (which is what I expected).   Since this Starbucks is so close to Costco and ALDI, I expect that normally it is more packed, but I'll do some field research and come back a couple more times. For Yelp, of course.

text: This is my go to coffee shop as it is near my house and I pass it every morning on the way to work.  The staff is friendly, and they generally keep a dark roast brewed throughout the whole day (one of my requirements!)  One thing to note is that if you are in a hurry and see 3 or more cars at the drive through, I recommend going inside to order instead.  You never know when you are going to get stuck behind someone ordering hot food and a few special venti double espresso, double non-fat, soy, mocha macchiatos.  You'll save quite a bit of time by ordering inside when stuff like this happens, especially if you have simple orders (ie standard coffee or espresso).

text: This review is specifically for January 11,2020. I (a disabled former RN whose boyfriend needs to drive me) go to this specific Starbucks 7 days a week and the majority of the time I just say my name at the drive thru and they say "Hey heath just pull around we got ur order."  On Jan 10, 2020 I forgot my phone with my bday free drink on it and instead used my Starbucks credit card.  I figured I'll just bring my phone on the 11th for the free drink.  On January 11,2020, they denied me using the coupon which was still on my Starbucks app with the redeem button still active. I told them that I simply forgot my phone, I'm a 7 day a week customer to the Warminster Starbucks and please honor the coupon. They said there was no possible way to acquiesce my request.  Considering the amount of money I spend at this establishment, the fact that I was only 1 day late, and the excellent relationships I have with most of the employees, I was very upset that they wouldn't honor my simple request.  If the 5-star employees who know me and my dog so well would have been working there on the 11th they would have taken care of me as one usually does to an extremely loyal long time customer.  All other days this place would have gotten 5 stars, but it's one star for the 11th of January if that's how they would so easily dismiss a long time loyal customer.   On showing up to Starbucks Warminster on January 12, 2020, Jaime the manager apologized said she saw the review and completely took care of me today. I thanked her and we had a lovely exchange. This is the employees that make this place awesome. I was so surprised at how fast she saw the above review and when I showed up today at the ordering screen it was Jaime and she immediately told me to drive around and she would take care of the issue plus honor my bday drink tomorrow. I purposely go to this Starbucks 7 days a week because of people like Jaime, John, and all the other employees that know me by name and we always have  a brief exchange of pleasantries at the drive through counter. They feel like family and I look forward to seeing them every single day.

text: This Starbucks is in the New Orleans Marriott at the Convention Center.  They have strange hours from 7am to 3pm.  So if you need an afternoon pick-me-up get there before 3pm otherwise you are out of luck.  I am glad this Starbucks was here because I don't think there were any other coffee shops within a few block radius.  The staff working there were super sweet - especially Beth, she's awesome!  I came here a lot during the week of Mardi Gras because I am a Meeting Planner and had a conference at the hotel that week.  The hotel gives you a mug that you can use while working that will give you unlimited whatever you  want refills at this Starbucks.  BRILLIANT!  Why don't more hotels do this? I love it!  The hotel staff was always asking me what I wanted and taking my mug to the Starbucks and filling it up with a soy latte or mocha.  They even washed it for me in the mornings!   The only downside are the limited hours, and when I came there once and asked if there were bagels or oatmeal they said no (like no as in they don't carry it) so I didn't have any breakfast options because I'm vegan.  However when my friend came like 5 days later and asked if there was oatmeal they had it.  Perhaps they were just out of it the day I asked but the way it came across was not that they had run out, but that they just didn't carry it.  Once I found out they had oatmeal I too got it.  As a vegan I can't eat their other pastries/breakfast items.  Also because it's in the hotel you don't get the waivers for using your registered Starbucks card like you do at other Starbucks.  Meaning that in most Starbucks if I pay for my latte with my card they waive the soy tax but this one doesn't.  So that makes all my drinks around $0.60 more than I usually pay. BOO SOY TAX.  Anyway this place is a life savor. I can't run a conference without a Starbucks.  It's seriously one of the most important features to a hotel if I am looking at a site.  Besides the few things mentioned above, it's just like any other Starbucks:  quick, friendly, convenient.

text: On the good - this Starbucks is HUGE, having started its life as a fast food seafood place (the one named after a pirate).  On the bad - the parking is a mere suggestion and it can be hard to get in and out of this location.  I've used the drive thru many times with no issues, but recently met up with a group of friends to play board games.  We took over the long table near the registers and had a fantastic time.  A few other customers stopped to ask about the games we were playing and we invited a few to join (they declined - maybe they saw the "serious gamer" gleam in our eyes).  It was a lovely afternoon.  My only complaint was that the air conditioning was set to slightly arctic.  However, I never travel without a hoodie so I survived pretty well.  Of course, the freezing temps gave me a chance to add that their restrooms were sparkly clean - so very devious, Starbucks, but thanks for keeping the loos in great shape!

text: This Starbucks is in a cute little part of Webster Groves next to Gazebo Park, where events and farmers markets are held in summer. It's just like any other Starbucks but this is right next to my work so I go there all the time, usually around 2:30-3pm when I hit the afternoon slump. The staff are friendly and always prompt with my order, and they rarely mess it up.  My go-to drinks are the white chocolate mocha with non-fat milk and no whipped cream, as well as the java chip frappacino, skinny and without whipped cream.  The clientele at this location are a mixture of business people, hippies, young teens (Nerinx Hall, an all-girls high school is just around the corner), university students (Webster University is within walking distance), artists, entrepreneurs, and stay-at-home moms. You really get a mixed bag here.  I typically don't sit and stay to drink my lattes, but when I have in the past, the furniture is comfy, with a variety of seating choices for individuals or small groups, and the place is kept clean and orderly. It doesn't wow my pants off, but it maintains the high standard of Starbucks that I expect. A solid standby.

text: This sleek modern architectural design of a Starbucks building, invites you to pull on in.  And if the Starbucks itself doesn't pull you in, then the connecting Chipotle may!   Yes, I'm a sucker for some Starbucks and Chipotle.  I don't think I've ever consumed the two in one sitting, but I'm open to the idea now.  Neighboring the Saint Louis University campus area, this Starbucks can become very busy at certain hours of the day.  Students flocking in for their caffeine fix and plopping down with their Mac Books.  That being said, I wouldn't count on every visit being a breeze.  You may end up waiting a little longer than had you choose a different location.    There's not a vast amount of parking available for the Starbucks in particular.  In fact, there's barely any.  You may need to end up parking in one of the neighboring lots, and turn an eye to the signs informing you not to park there.  Hey, sometimes the rules need to be broken in the name of a latte.  Luckily, there is also the drive thru option available at this location, which nicely wraps around the building.  *Drink of the moment: Iced Soy 2 Pump Sugar Free Cinnamon Dolce Latte.  Is that a mouth full, or what?  Yeah, a mouth full of deliciousness!

text: This is a smallish Starbuck's compared to some in town but it is my local one and is perfect for the slightly out-of-the way, family oriented, slightly rural feeling Mesa neighborhood of Santa Barbara. There are a few chairs and small tables on the "pet friendly" front patio that is in an alcove sheltered from the weather. The atmosphere inside is cozy on a cold day and, being near SB city college, is often quiet and studious but is friendly and comfortable. There are two (quite well maintained) bathrooms which is unusual for a small coffeehouse, and the staff is quite efficient and friendly though the place does occaisionally get very busy in the morning rush.  This is a "big chain" coffee with a distinctly friendly, local ambience!

text: You know how women always make jokes about how men are extremely sexually unsatisfying in the bedroom? Well, let's just say Starbucks is a man and I'm his woman.  It started out one morning as a beautiful and fantastical idea - maybe even kinky. Go to Starbucks, enjoy a nice hot cup of coffee and slob on some spectacular oatmeal. Sadly, this fantasy was never realized.  As I pulled into the drive thru, I glanced begrudgingly at the seemingly endless queue of cars lining the asphalt covered pavement. Although the thought of waiting in this line seemed almost as pressing as waiting for the universe to end, I decided that maybe the coffee would be worth it. Spoiler alert: Twasn't.   My tires squeaked as they fought with the pavement leading to the drive thru speaker:  "I would like an Oat Milk Latte please, no foam - and make that unsweetened."  Like songs from a siren, these simple words floated effortlessly from my mouth. However, I had to repeat myself multiple times. I felt as though I was screaming at a brick wall. Eventually, my order was completed and I was able to get through to the worker: now to wait the rest of the way.  I graciously drove my vehicle to the drive thru window:  "Apple Pay?"  The worker asked, to which I replied:  "Yes."  I paid for my (or so I thought) delicious order. However, as soon as my iPhone 12 Plus grazed the very last atoms of the card reader, I was handed a drink - but not mine. Confused, shocked, concerned, and unsettled, I begged for the coffee I so struggled to order.  After another order confirmation (one of trillions), I finally received my latte. Delight! Cream! Delish! WRONG! WRONG! My hastiness got the better of me. As I pulled away from this establishment, I noticed a few things about my drink.  1) There seemed to be foam. 2) I was unsure if it was even oat milk. 3) It didn't bring me pure ecstasy and joy.  This was truly a devastating blow to my ego. Upset and belittled, I chose to move on and order my coffee elsewhere. At the end of the day, I learned a lesson. To never come back to this Starbucks. Have a wonderful evening.

text: Be prepared to be underwhelmed and even slightly dissatisfied.    I am without a doubt a very loyal Starbucks customer and have been for years. To the point that I definitely have my local to me locations but I stop by on a daily basis. This does not make me an expert, but it does make me a consistent consumer who is knowledgeable about Starbucks products and services.   This location is located inside of the GSR hotel in Reno, and is frequented by predominantly hotel guests and staff. I can't imagine someone stopping by the hotel just to go to this specific Starbucks.    The seating area is ridiculous. While it is open seating, it's located near the check in/out counters, as well as, gambling sections. It's just not a great spot/location overall.    The service is close to horrendous, and the staff is just the same.   I ordered two different drinks, both fairly easy to make, and I was the second person in line, with no one else behind me. My drinks took close to 25 minutes to be made. Yes, you read that correctly, 25 minutes for two drinks and no one else was waiting.  Once the drinks were ready, despite the fact that I was standing fairly close to the counter waiting, the barista put them on the counter and walked away. Did not call out my name, let me know that the drinks were mine versus maybe a mobile order, etc. then to top it off, both drinks were made incorrectly. Unfortunately, I did not discover this until I had already left the counter and was on my way outside of the hotel. No one wants to trek back into a hotel that is currently under construction, and frankly has become a hot mess to get in and out of. And let's face it, who wants to deal with unpleasant people when they are handling our food and or beverages?!    Would  I stop by this location again? More than likely only under desperation.  My two stars are because if nothing else, the seating area and counters are clean.

Apparently they filled this thing to the brim while vertical which caused it to overflow and almost become pressurized once placed in its normal horizontal position.  I've ordered these travelers dozens of times, from dozens of different Starbucks across the US and never had this happen. On an extremely busy convention weekend, leaving new hires to fend for themselves is pretty messed up for them AND for us.  My hand is throbbing and swelling so I'd like to give negative stars and even sue for that. But I'll get over it once the pain stops! text: I kind of want to give this Starbucks three stars, but it's thanksgiving... I'll give an extra one, and here's why...  The location is great. I happen to live in the area, and there isn't much here at all - so they have the monopoly. The staff is very friendly, and they usually are pretty well staffed. It's inside the Westin hotel. For me, parking isn't an issue because I usually walk there, but there are only three or four parking spots. I have occasionally stopped there on my way to work and asked the valet guys if I could just pull up. They are very generous and allow for that.  The downside - at times they can be a little slow. Since they're in a hotel, they'll often get large orders, and I've been behind that large order. No fault of theirs, but beware! Also, at times they don't adhere to their hours, and they don't have the "typical" Starbucks hours.   All things considered, this is a great Starbucks for people on rocky point!

text: I want this Starbucks to be great because of its convenient location, but it's only okay! Plenty of outlets, but bring a jacket because it will be chilly.  This is a rather large Starbucks, and definitely one that a lot of people frequent when they're in the Camino Real area shopping. There are several seats, including a long raw wood top table beside the windowed wall that faces Los Agaves. During peak times, there's probably only 2-3 tables open if you need to work. It's normally pretty cold in here, so bring a jacket if you plan on staying for a while.  The cleanliness of this store is always what gets me. It seems very unkempt and dirty, and that has stopped me from working here a time or two. The bathroom is always in bad shape. I'd recommend using the Camino Real public restrooms outside - they are MUCH better!  But Starbucks is still tempting. When I know I can order ahead from the app and skip a line, access an outlet and work all afternoon, have access to real food and not just pastries at a coffee shop, this is a great option. It's in a great spot, making grocery shopping before or after i work a lot easier. Even still, I much prefer other Starbucks locations compared to this one.

text: You know,  not all Starbucks are created equal hence why they have 4 on each corner of the street.  You don't like one,  go to another,  right? Well, I would unless I was at a conference and not trying to walk in the rain. So, the only good thing about this Starbucks is that it's connected to the convention center via a skywalk.  So, I trudge over there each morning and order the same thing, soy latte no foam. No biggie, no major frills but I guess these baristas do not understand no foam nor filling it up to the top.   I normally order a tall and have gotten used to the weight of my drink. So when I order a grande, due to the long conference,  it should weigh much more since it goes from 12 to 16 oz. It SHOULD but it doesn't. In fact, I think it almost weighed less if that's possible. I opened my cup to take a gander and the first thing I noticed was how low the foam came up my cup. I would say it was about an inch below the top.  Then, why is there sooo much foam? I understand that soy is different and it will foam later once it settles but seriously,  I do not think this barista did anything different from normal.  So, I went back up and asked her kindly to add some more soymilk since it was not full. Well, she did but she filled it with the cold soymilk. So I asked if she could steam it first. Hmm...so when she does pour it in, I swear she poured in about 4 oz more of soymilk.  So, if you must get to a Starbucks AND you're at the convention center and want convenience,  this'll do.

text: Hey it's a Starbucks. Hard to tell them apart except for the staff. They do a rotation between Newtown and Heacock road. Starbucks embodies the ultimate McDonaldlization of society. Regardless of your staff you can always for the most part get what you want and it taste fairly similar. I.e. friers on the right levers on the left. So what I'm saying for an organization that is not franchised, who really runs them. Typically it's going to be your students and possibly mid level managers. Customer service is paramount. I think the idea for many customers is they like to build relationships and see that familiar face at each location consistently of the staff.  I've noticed that both customers and employees seem to be discouraged when they are store hopping. It feels good when you walk in welcomed by your first name and they know what your order is. I think that a Starbucks cared they would stop the movement and allow those relationships to form. Their sales would increase their ability to upsell would increase as well. It's all about relationships folks. I give three stars not because of the staff because of the executive management that does not value this type of relationship. I believe training is the underlining cause. Which probably breeds inefficiencies if someone does not want to move as the incentive is to excel to run the store. But this is only for the short term as Starbucks has a high attrition rate as this is only a stepping stone.

text: Speedy service, Keep it up baristas! always very friendly and professional.  A little facts on Starbucks...originally, Starbucks offered only two sizes: short (8 ounces) and tall (12 ounces). When the company added grande, venti and trenta, they kept the old names for the original drinks.  At most Starbucks around the world, the size options are: short (8 ounces), tall (12 ounces), grande (16 ounces), venti (24 ounces), and trenta (31 ounces).  Grande is Italian for 'large,' venti means 'twenty,' and trenta is 'thirty'.   After a trip to Italy in 1983, founder Howard Schultz became obsessed with its coffee bars and wanted to introduce Italian names. As customers demanded larger sizes, 'short' was taken off the menu board so the larger sizes of 'venti' and 'trenta' sizes could be added Tall ended up becoming the smallest sized drink on the printed menu.

text: There are only a few people in the world that I would sit in Starbucks for over two hours waiting for, and one of them happens to be my best friend from college.  So as i sat there drinking my $4.00 cup of coffee and $2 cookie and grading TONS of papers, i had a lot of time to make observations about the people hanging out at a random starbucks on a saturday afternoon  Scenario #1:  The first Blind Date The couple right next to me were very clearly on a first (and most likely blind) date.  This was the interaction i heard.  "Uh, so you're an engineer. Do you like it?" "Yea.  You're an accountant.  Do you like it?" "Yea.  You live in Plymouth Meeting?  Do you LIke it?"   And this continued for almost an hour.   And believe it or not i think they actually liked the date!  Starbucks is clearly an awesome meeting point for a blind date on a saturday afternoon, since its public and not too romantic, but it definitely is not something i need to listen to  Scenario #2:  The teenage boys being jerks This is self explanatory.   They were blaring music, screaming at their iPod touches and casually glancing at their trophy wife mom's drinking coffee in the main area.    Finally i shot my "teacher look" and they shut up  Scenario #3: The Teacher I was not the only person in the place grading papers.  There was someone i believe was a college professor due to the size of their research papers.   She was very focused, but also annoyed at the teenage boys.  Then there was even a retired teacher who approached me and talked to me (i guess the papers were a dead give away!)  I definitely was able to focus and now think maybe Starbucks might have to start being a location for me to get some work done  Overall, i still think Starbucks is overpriced and i could have done without the christmas music in March.   But i do like that they had an upstairs which is a lot quieter to get some work done... as long as there are no teenage boys!

text: Oh dear god ... if you go to a "regular" Starbucks (i.e. not on the U of A campus), you'll have an awesome visit and it'll be everything Starbucks is suppose to be.  If you go to a Starbucks on the U of A campus, you'll probably have a terrible experience, especially at this location, right outside the Cameron Library.  First off, the servers only speak spanish.  Why?  I have no idea.  They also never smile or strike up a normal, polite conversation with you.  The one server who must be a manager of sorts always looks like she's ready to kill someone.  I actually don't think she's capable of a smile or even standard conversation.  The drinks and food are the same as any other Starbucks, and you early points on your Starbucks membership at this location, but you can't use your points at this location.  This is stupid and I think has something to do with how this location is franchised compared to others.  I worked at the library and in the summer this location didn't open until 8am, the same time the library opened.  For students, this is fine, but for staff it sucked.  If they even opened 15 minutes earlier it would have made life so much easier.  And in the summer their sign says they close at 3:30pm.  Personally I think that's ridiculously early for a coffee shop.  And for awhile their sign said 4pm, but they still closed at 3:30pm!  They're also incredibly slow.  In fact, I think I could be faster moving backwards.  They always seem to have a line, but it's mainly because they serve so slowly! text: Maybe it's because this Starbucks feels right at home nestled neatly on the boutique corridor of Illinois and 56th, maybe's it's because there's no drive-through or busy highway to deal with, or maybe it has that certain coffee shop feel that you get from the depths of a city neighborhood, instead of a strip-mall.  Whatever it is, I like this version of Starbucks.  It's pretty straight-forward; we all know what we get - tall, grande, venti, grip - frap, whip, skinny, sip.  It's quality bean at a fair price, that's why we all go.  So what does this leave me to review?  Service, of course.  These baristas are some of the friendliest around; every time I stop in, they are very welcoming and put me at ease from my chronic condition of running late for work - at least if I'm generalizing about myself...  Parking can get a little crammed on this side of town.  There's not much room behind the boutiques and there's limited curb-side spots.  There is outdoor seating when the weather permits, which is great for people watching  This is a great little pedestrian/bike friendly coffee shop in Butler-Tarkington, one I can raise my cup to.

text: Everyone is complaining about the parking.  Me too.  The issue is not just that there's only a tiny lot, but that the surrounding streets all have parking bans or permit parking only, so there's nowhere to go for overflow parking.  The parking lot is too tiny to reasonably fill this coffee shop, assuming about 2 people to a car.  There's ample seating but barely any parking.  There isn't even any METERED parking nearby.  But to add insult to injury, this place isn't exactly easily accessible by ANY form of transportation.  I'm a walker, and a SEPTA user.  This location is along a big street with fast traffic, and the lot is between the sidewalk and the door...not friendly to walkers.  It's very near the SEPTA station but the walk from the station to the Starbucks isn't a friendly one.  Other than that, this place is decent.  Nice employees, decent decor.  But seriously...I'd like to see them do something about making this location more friendly to people trying to actually GET to it, whether by car or by foot.

text: I come here almost every morning and usually leave with a smile on my face! The baristas are always so friendly and if you go often enough, they will start to remember you and possibly your drink order if you don't change up too often! I use the drive thru on my way to work which usually take around 10 minutes start to finish around 9:30am.   There are a ton of cars they service at this time so all in all, the wait isn't all the bad. You could always park and go onside to order but that would take just as long in my opinion.   My drink order is always right and is always consistent no matter what I order. I do change my order frequently so consistency is key and they do a really good job of this. My go to is pretty simple, iced latte. But, I love to try all of their seasonal beverages. I recommend the pumpkin spice cold brew which is new this year. Just a hint of pumpkin so more for everyday drinking where as the traditional pumpkin spice latte is so sweet (and delicious) but I couldn't drink it everyday. They also have seasonal food items which I still haven't tried yet but plan to!   My favorite breakfast items are the egg bites (any flavor) and the bacon and Gouda breakfast sandwich. That is the bomb dot com. You can go wrong when trying one of these to kick start your day with flavor plus they are so filling and it last all the way until lunch! text: This Starbucks is never open on time and ready to serve it's customers.   I use to be able to depend on them to be open in the morning before I go to work to get my $8.00 coffee and scone. I have counted as many as 9 people waiting to have coffee outside their door, at 6 am . It must be costly to the owner to loose sales, like this. The customers are walking next door, to a little pastry shop for their morning cup of coffee and pastry. Honestly the pastries next door are much better. PJ's is there but does not open till 6:30 am.  It is awful to drive to a place for coffee, be in a rush because you have to be at  work, and not have the doors, open. So irritating to say the least. No one smile or greets you and in fact you get ignored for the most part while they stock the pastry in the display.   I would like to know who is responsible to open, and if the owner knows, they  are loosing sales and customers. I myself am there every Friday, Saturday, and Sunday before work at 6am and spend an average of 8-10 dollars a day.  That is 30 dollars a week some 1500 - 1600 a year. I cannot tolerate this  inconsistency and will take my money eslewhere.   I am just infuriated by the whole thing.

text: If this were your first experience of the Starbucks franchise, you would walk away neither overly impressed nor depressed.  For those of us who've easily been in dozens of them, the feeling is about the same. In our particular case this week, it was a necessary evil -- we were trying to locate yet another tourist attraction (this sounds like complaining, but trust me, we were having a fabulous time) and the iPhone and its handy GPS were nearly dead, so we ducked into Starbucks for a drink and a good long battery charge.   I'd never had a Starbucks smoothie before and I'll never have one again. It was just not like a 'real' smoothie -- no fruit chunks, no bold fruit flavors, nothin' interesting, and a strong preservative taste to seal the deal. text: I've been to this location about a dozen times. My husband and I enjoy going in on a rainy day and reading for a bit. The inside it pretty big and they have outdoor seating but it's usually pretty crowded. Unfortunately the last 2/3 visits have been negative. We stopped on a Sunday morning before church and went in because the drive-thru line was super long. I ordered the same thing for both myself and my husband. It took 15 minutes for our drinks to come out. Not kidding. I requested the drinks have sugar-free syrup because of dietary restrictions. We rushed out once the drinks were made and didn't try them until we got to our car and were out of the parking lot. One of the drinks was SF and the other wasn't. We had to throw the one with regular syrup away and couldn't go back because our drinks took so long. The woman who made them was clearly flustered because they were super busy but she made them back-to-back. That mistake shouldn't have been made.  Fast forward to this morning at 6:50 am. No one was in the drive-thru. I ordered my drink (the same one that was messed up previously) and gave the woman my money. A man made my drink and set it on the counter and then went to the back, I think. The woman also disappeared. Literally. FOUR MINUTES PASSED and my drink was sitting there. She came back to take someone else's order in the drive-thru and then proceeded to get a cup and start making their drink. I kindly said "excuse me, I have somewhere I need to be. Can you please give me my drink?" She didn't say anything and just handed it to be. I was in shock. I'm not kidding when I say it was four whole minutes. If I could've reached in the window and grabbed the drink myself I would have.  I tell this long story because both of these poor visits involved the same woman. A little bit older with short blonde hair. She can be very nice but tends to be a little out of it at times. I hate to call someone specific out but in both accounts she was the one to blame. I still plan on visiting this location but hope that this particular employee gets the hang of her job.

text: I have been to all over the country, and have never felt I needed to write a review for one unit now.   When my friend and I walked into the place they had a large display right in the doorway [blocking everything, so clearly they would like you to see it.] with their new refreshers in a can. When I picked one up to look at it, the cashier from a distance shouted to us "Those are gross. The ones we make are ok, but those ones are gross."   Ummmm...OK.  We approach the counter to place our orders and the girl walks away. We stand there for several minutes waiting for someone to ask us what we wanted to order.. but nothing.   THERE WERE 7 PEOPLE WORKING BEHIND THE COUNTER.  Once we did put in the order we ended up waiting15 minutes for it. There were no drinks they needed to make in front of ours. They were all standing around talking. The funny thing there was what appeared to be a trainer behind the counter teaching a new employee to do something.  The man that ordered behind us didnt get eye contact, asked questions that were answered with short snotty answers and was treated even worse.  We overheard conversations about employees and their medications, weekend plans, hair products, etc.   I am still shocked about the lack of proffesionalism and the dirtiness of the store, but what blows me away the most is that the drinks took 15 minutes to make. I live in Chicago and watch a team of 2 take care of a line of 30 people with no problems. These 7 people couldn't get an iced drink together. GET IT TOGETHER.

text: I love Starbucks. Forget what people say about Dunkin' Donuts--that's bilge water! I don't even drink coffee anymore, but they offer an array of herbal teas iced and hot, and lately I've been treating myself to dark chocolate-covered graham crackers. Don't judge.  That being said, I must concur with many Yelp reviewers in noting that this branch is THE most inefficient location I've ever frequented. First, although the line is routinely interminably long, only ONE register is open. Employees appear to be milling about in the background doing who knows what. True, other baristas are busy filling ridiculously complicated drink orders ("Ummmm...I'll have a half-caf iced Americano with a splash of coconut milk, a smidge of soy, and the tears of the woman behind me in line who's screaming and pulling her hair out."), but still.   The other day, two middle-aged women conferred with the cashier for 15 minutes as I waited (uncharacteristically) my turn patiently. Were they opening an offshore bank account, transferring money from their hedge fund to checking? No; for the life of me, I have no clue what the heck was happening, despite my earnest attempts at eavesdropping. Variations of this scenario are repeated daily, but this is the location closest to me.  The workers here are very friendly, and I usually tip, but I take serious umbrage with the extremely poor service. I kind of enjoy getting worked up about stuff, so I'll totally continue to patronize this Starbucks, but still...

text: I am thankful that I have a Starbucks close by my house for those mornings that I wake up and just don't want to make coffee. It's not often that you find a Starbucks where the employees aren't friendly and the coffee impeccable and this Starbucks is no different. I've walked in, mobile ordered and ask gone through the drive thru and each time the service has been amazing and quick. The only downside to this Starbucks has nothing to do with the service or the flavorful awakening coffee they serve but instead the layout. There are a couple of ways to enter this drive thru and neither flows nicely. If you are coming from the front of the store it's hard not to block traffic while waiting to enter the drive thru line and I've been cut in line a few times by drivers that just come around behind me apparently thinking I'm waiting on something else? Or they just cut you off coming from the backside. At this one, if there is a line I'll usually mobile order and it's literally ready within minutes. I've ordered from the line for the drive thru before and by the time I got around, parked and walked inside it was sitting waiting for me. Nothing negative to say about this place....Thank you for quenching my coffee cravings in a jiffy Starbucks....and doing it with a pretty amazing smile!

text: I love this Starbucks!  I agree with some other reviewers about the decor not being as fancy or modern as other Starbucks, but I don't really care about the decorations as much as the customer service.  We ALL know that Starbucks isn't cheap, but when you have great service it feels worth it.   Consequently - I've had HORRIBLE service at the one across the street in La Cumbre Plaza....literally the barista called out "Venti iced coffee sweetened for Mary" and I stood up and apologetically gestured "Oh, I said nosweetener actually" and she glared at me while dumping out my iced coffee (continuously glaring at me) and then refilled it and pushed it towards me.  Her coworker was standing next to her and I smiled at her and said "Wow, what great customer service, huh?" She just smiled and looked away.  I mean, if you hate your job it definitely shows through to your customers.  I've never had a bad experience here at this Starbucks, but I avoid the other like the plague.  I come for the coffee but I come back for the excellent service.  In fact, at this one in particular I've had baristas tell me that if I'm not happy with my drink choice to please let them know so they can remake it.

text: "Wedges, edges, and ledges"  They typical Starbucks easily gets 3 stars.  It's rare to find one that earns 5 stars, at least from me.  For this Northwest Reno Starbucks, which I once upon a time visited regularly, their breaking of the template from vapid strip mall digs into varied looking clusters of retail pads with different geometric shapes makes for a more interesting store. I'm talking about a vaulted, open ceiling once inside.  And a floor plan that isn't squarish.  And all kinds of soffits and ledges that make it even niftier.  The product is standard Starbucks fare and, yes, I've noticed that their prices have gone up some.  That medium sized (grande, I believe) cup of hot tea now costs a little more ... at all Starbucks stores.  However, I came in to find the same enthusiastic and prompt service I was used to getting.  There is an area at its perimeter with floor to ceiling windows if you want more of that high desert light.  (There are also adjustable shades.)  And there's an area closer to the bathrooms that is tucked away and has some big upholstered chairs.  It's all good.  The bathrooms are typically in clean enough and usable shape.  There is also a small outside seating area and, on occasion, I've seen people bring their canine friends and, with the typically dry conditions, it's understandable why they would.  There is a parking lot that is fairly spacious and, behind the ridge that this shopping area is perched on, there is a steep ravine.  Who knows what the hell lives down here?  I'm thinking coyotes and snakes, at the very least.  But this Starbucks and adjacent stores feel very apart from that wild world below, allowing you to experience a suburban coffeehouse experience that doubles as suburban Californian.  This Starbucks is just different enough on various levels that, with my 4 word tag line to describe this set-up - wedges, edges, and ledges, it's a 4 star Starbucks.  And I'm glad I dropped by to see that things hadn't changed.

text: Nothing but terrible experiences at this location! I have never had a smooth and efficient experience while visiting this Starbucks, both in the drive thru and in the store. The drive thru is terribly slow with ridiculous wait times of upwards 15 minutes. I've pulled up to the window on several occasions where a barista has even opened the window to greet me or on other occasions they will open the window, process my payment, and then disappear for several minutes without communication what is going on. Let's talk about the inside and recap my episode from this morning. There must have been three baristas working drive through, two working the bar, one working the till, and a barista (who I later found out was the manager) was running around providing support where needed. It was absolute chaos. After waiting in line for 15-20 minutes I waited around for my sandwich and pastry. I communicated to the barista at the till that I was going to wait off to the left for my items. I don't think he understood what I meant, none the less I waited for an additional five minutes before asking the barista, who I later found out was the manager, about the status of my order. She stated that they were down at the end of the bar and that they had called my name several minutes ago. I was standing RIGHT THERE. No one ever called my name. I was already late for work and left. As I was pulling out of the plaza, my sandwich didn't smell right. I looked and it was the WRONG sandwich. They gave me sausage, which I cannot eat. I had to drive back and run in with the sandwich.The manager first verified the name on the bag before confirming that indeed I was given the wrong sandwich. She re-made the sandwich and asked for which form of payment I used and offered me a refund, not understanding that a refund would be more inconvenient for me as I would have to go back to my car and get my wallet. Let's be real, they have free drink cards. She could have provided better customer service. Unless this Starbucks issues an apology I will not be visiting it anytime soon. What should have been a convenience ended up in a 30 minute experience due to complete chaos behind the counter and poorly run service.

text: Maple is better than Magazine.  Metarie is better than Maple.  That being said, Maple is the location I visit most often as it is closest to my home.  I have been visiting this Starbucks more so  in the past few months.  I am tired of paying for just OK coffee at the local chains- PJ's, CC's, and on occasion Rue and Zots.  I don't include Village Coffee, because I only go there for 1 thing.   But bad cup after bad cup being thrown in the streets of New Orleans by this coffee loving woman, I could be polluting the Lake by now with crappy cups of coffee.  Not so with Starbucks.   THIS location is a 4.  They, for a long time, would "fix" coffee made by lesser Starbucks, (I'm looking at YOU Magazine Street).  They really have their act together.  I actually "crave" their coffees.  I also like the treat receipt program which gets you a two dollar medium(there, I said it), after 2 PM that same day.  I also use  their card, belong in their club, and buy gift certificates on line cheap when I can.  There are ways to make this  Starbucks habit affordable.      The one evening they were closed at 9 PM, and needed a fix, I thought about my options in the area, drove down to BK and got a Seattle's Best. (Which was better than some other local places).  There are those who will disagree with me. I am ready for that.  I was also ready to stop wasting my money at places just because they were local, and not enjoying their products.  It all makes perfect sense, it just took a long time.

text: I doubt this review would ever be a candidate for a Review of the Day, but who knows, there's always a first time for anything, right?  In any case, while visiting my former college roommate in the Irish Channel last month, I was comforted to see this Starbucks a couple of blocks away on Washington and Magazine Street.  Surprisingly, I found locating Starbuckses, in general, to be difficult in New Orleans, especially considering that there are tons of really nice cafes and independently owned joints in the French Quarter and along Magazine Street. This particular Starbucks, however, served as a convenient road marker to know that I was on track to finding the St. Charles trolley a couple more blocks down on Washington Street.  I ended up taking a small detour here while walking to the trolley in the late morning on a weekday, only to find that the place was full of people. Lots of people who work immediately around the area, as well as students (your typical workday crowd) patronized the place.  Coming from Los Angeles for vacation, I did notice that I was the only one without a stack of papers, laptops or a backpack that morning. In a way, I was happy I was the only one without worry about work or studying and sat around observing the New Orleans/Southern style architecture here at this Starbucks. And, as is the beauty of franchises: you get a consistent product no matter where you are, and the hazelnut macchiato here was no exception and tasted as fine as anywhere else.  The baristas here provided a high level of customer service, as I noticed that they were keeping busy sweeping the floors in the main dining room area when there was finally a lull in the queue. In a separate incident, one of them didn't flinch or give a weird look at a patron who returned a partially drunk coffee. In fact, they were really happy about replacing the drink. The patron may or may not have realized that it was not to her liking until about halfway through, and most people, if they were in the barista's shoes would probably have made a look, gossip later or show a hint of attitude. None here...and I was impressed at the restraint and professionalism.  The setup of this particular Starbucks is elongated. The front desk is parallel to Washington Street (the long side of the rectangle shape of the coffee house's layout). There's a bit of an issue with seating arrangements inside because it gets pretty narrow as you walk towards the rear of the store (as if you're walking down the store with Washington Street to your right). If it gets a bit too claustrophobic for you, you can always do what many others did that day and take a seat outside. Thankfully, I like to think I brought the California weather with me that week, so it was totally enjoyable to actually sit outside in a typically hot and humid time of year.

text: I'm not a frequent coffeehouse patronizer, but this Starbucks is one of my go-to locations because its close to work. Located on the ground floor of the Marriott, this Starbucks has a very corporate vibe to it. There is always a ton of people in here, with pretty significant lines.   This is not a cozy-feeling Starbucks at all, being that interior space is very tight--like a thin, lengthy road. The amount of seating is sparse, and rather disjointed (a couple alcoves and a bar-type window seating area). This is not a place that you would wish to spend any length of time in.  However, you can be pretty sure of one thing - your food & drink will be fresh, being that there is a high volume of people that pass through here. In fact, I *highly* recommend that you get the Starbucks app to order/pay so that you can skip the crazy lines.   Theresa may be the best barista in town - we're talking Best of Philly. She obviously works hard, she always has my drinks made perfectly, and she is one of the nicest ladies in town. I think she is only here in the late morning, though.  I tried ordering through the app just yesterday, and it all worked flawlessly for me. I ordered and paid with my mobile phone, it gave me an estimated time of 3-8 minutes that my order would be read, I walked over to the Starbucks, and by golly I had my drink in-hand on the 8th minute!  Why this location is not open 24 hours, is beyond me; apparently Philly cannot sustain a 24 hour Starbucks. If any location was going to be 24 hours, I'd think this would have been it.

text: -- COVID-19 review --  I've visited this Starbucks several, many, beaucoup times before and I'm not sure how I didn't write a review, but here goes nothing...  Just to save time, I ordered ahead, using the app, I love this order ahead feature... I love, love, love this feature. I was using it before Covid-19. I love it, I  really, really do; do you think I love it? You're right, I do.   I ordered a Venti green tea Matcha Frappuccino with coconut milk please, because your girl is lactose intolerant, but I still wanna get my matcha on, so there you have it coconut milk it is. I don't like that they up charge you $.60 for coconut milk, I mean don't charge me because I have a genetic in-deficiency, hello. At any rate...  I entered the facility and per City of NOLA Covid regulations, I had my mask on. I walked to the counter to pick up my drink, they verified me, I picked up my drink & I proceeded to leave.   The barista's hold the drinks behind the counter because I believe there's been some issues with folks doing a 'snatch & grab' of drinks they didn't order or pay for. It's all good for me, no hassle that just reassures me that my drink is super fresh and hasn't been tampered with; DON'T SLIP ME NO MICKIE'S.   I tipped them on the app on my way out the door. I was happy, I received super fast service, the baristas were polite, they greeted me as I entered & asked if I wanted a straw: CLASSIC STARBUCKS BEHAVIOR.   Annnnnndddddd my drink tasted exactly as it should, coconut milk and all... I was very pleased about this because I ordered a Venti so there was lots of DRINK to be DRUNK.   Clean facilities, inside & outside. I was able to do my normal stick and move caper... I was in & I was outta there in a jiffy.   VERY limited street parking.

text: First, I will start out saying that what I'm writing here reflects my views of most Starbucks locations that I visit however, in all fairness to the establishment I won't say they're all like this.   Consistently inconsistent due to time of day, location, and barista I feel.   I come here quite often as it's not outside my normal circle of travel. I decided to go with two stars on the rating because I'm not really happy with my drinks here. I feel like the only time my order is correct, is at night when it's slow. I've noticed that the barista's will intentionally make my order wrong if I've ordered something they don't agree with or they feel they are pressed for time. My drink usually isn't filled to the top either. So not only is my drink wrong but it's less than what I've paid for as well.   On many occasions I've had to argue to get my drink remade. Strange considering "The barista promise" advertised on their cups.   All in all the people are mostly friendly but their service and attention to detail lacks. I don't mind waiting for good quality service and a correct order but I get really frustrated with the opposite and an excuse attached. Sadly, I've come to expect my order to be wrong at this location. As we all know, it's easy to see our orders being made from the drive thru in any standard size vehicle or while standing inside, my accusations of my drink being incorrect don't come from me guessing. Let's not forget my taste buds know too.  I don't want this to be all bad so I will end with this- the location has great customer and is extremely clean. About three days ago they did give me two free drinks because I forgot my wallet and the Starbucks app wasn't working properly for me to pay using my phone. Thank you for that.

text: I am a regular at this Starbucks because it happens to be on my way to and from work every day.  There are many reasons why I continue to come back other then the fact of convenience, those being;  The barista's are always so friendly and polite; every time I've gone, they're smiling and casually making polite chatter with the customers.  The service is generally very prompt even during the busy morning hours.  When they do fall behind, they always apologize and sometimes depending on how backed up they are and how long you have been waiting, they give you your drink on the house as an apology.  This Starbucks has either drop in or drive thru so it's fairly convenient during the winter season when you don't really feel like getting out into the cold to drop in.  They also serve a variety of different delectable treats and delicious foods.  I've only had one "bad service" with this location and that was that I ordered a venti, paid for a venti and was given a grande. I contacted them and advised the mistake and was apologized to profusely and mailed coupons.  I appreciated that they took notice of their mistake & promptly fixed it.

text: I'm actually an employee at this particular Starbucks and just wanted to shed some light on some of the reviews and comments and concerns of our fellow customers.   MOBILE ORDERS:  I want to start off with our mobile ordering system. now typically with this of course it is meant to be quicker and your order is meant to be ready at your time of arrival. Im aware that the app gives you a time in which your order should be done, IGNORE this. It is not accurate and doesn't account for the large volume of mobile order drinks that we receive at one time. Majority of the time 10-15 mobile orders come though at ONE time. We are THE busiest in all of Tampa so sometimes there WILL be a wait. We (most days) receive enough at one time to stretch to our drive they window! YIKES! I know but trust your barista is busting their butt to assure we get your order our to you ASAP. 9/10 if your order is missing it is because you have not hit the very last button that finalizes your order and sends it to our location. If not it may be an error with our machine and simply letting your barista know at the bar that your order is missing we will be MORE than happy to make your drink on the fly. It's never a problem. Our baristas bust their butt daily to assure you all are taken care of the best way we can!    BATHROOMS: Secondly I noticed a review saying we lock our bathrooms. We do not lock the bathrooms. IF the bathroom IS locked, that simply means it is currently OCCUPIED.   REUSABLE CUPS:  While we do fill reusable cups, there IS a STANDARD in which drinks need to be made. If you have a sparkly reusable cup, it will not have the line measurements we NEED BY STANDARD to make sure we are giving you the proper proportions to build your beverage. While some are upset that we still use a cup other than your own to build the beverage, customers in drive thru expect there beverage to be ready upon arrival at the window. If our line is wrapped around the building and we have to wait for you to come to the window, take your cup and THEN make your drink, it not only will hold up the line but will make you getting your beverage a longer process.   COFFEE: During the morning we have all three coffees brewed. We have Pike which is our everyday blend, our dark roast which we switch every so often with a new flavor and our blonde roast. Obviously during the early morning hours demand for brewed coffee is much higher, therefore having all three coffees brewed is necessary. At ELEVEN is when we cut out the dark roast and the blonde because the demand for hot brewed coffee at that time isn't as high. Your barista will let you know (if it is after 11) that we no longer have blonde or dark, and then ask if you would like our house blend of Pike instead or wait about 3-4 minutes for a fresh POUROVER of dark or blonde roast coffee. We completely understand that we are a coffee shop therefore we should always have coffee, but we have found that we waste tons of coffee brewing all three that aren't as popular during later in the afternoon than our house blend coffee. Why brew something hardly anyone is ordering, which thus creates waste.  Hope this has helped some. If you have stopped coming to this location months ago I encourage you to give us another try as we are under new management.

text: Horrible.... This store started off great, but has drastically declined in both service and quality in the last week or so. I stopped by this store on Saturday, and today, and ordered a venti Marble Mocha Macchiato with whipped cream, which is made the following way: White mocha on the bottom, steamed milk, shots of espresso, then mocha drizzle on top. It is normally made without whipped cream, but I ask them to add it. Anyway, both Saturday and today I was given a caramel macchiato with no whipped cream. Since when did lying to a customer become good customer service? I asked her to have the manager call me, and she took down my information. Then she asked what the nature of the call was about. Anyway, now the Starbucks employee has lied to me, and argued with me over the phone. I seem to recall most companies having a policy "the customer is always right". Needless to say, I will not be going back to this location ever again. I think I will stick to PJs coffee, and hopefully everyone else will too, so we can run this establishment into the ground.

Whether it's cracking a joke with a customer to make them laugh, telling a customer about a latest deal, or just going the extra mile in general, the service has always been phenomenal. To be honest, it's probably the friendliest Starbucks I've ever been to.  Lastly, they're super efficient. Even when there's a long line, I never feel like I'm waiting too long, and my order has never been messed up. And to be honest, even if they did ever mess it up, I wouldn't even be mad because I know they'd handle it professionally. Best Starbucks ever!

This was not ok.

Mobile ordering said it wasn't available so I hopped in the car and when I got there the lights were out and employees were walking to their cars. I don't know if it's a communication issue and they're hours are incorrectly posted or what but I was thoroughly irritated. I will probably give them another go down the line I hopes that things have gotten sorted out but in the meantime, I'll drive a longer distance to avoid frustration.  All that being said, the staff have always been super nice so I'm chalking it up to logistical and training issues rather than quality of staff or competency.

text: I've driven past this Starbucks many times and thought it looked like an interesting place. I've never stopped, but today I found myself downtown and thought I should stop in and have a look.  Overall I'd say it's a pretty nice Starbucks, but it suffers from one fatal flaw as a work location (unless you live nearby) and that is the pay parking. I didn't spend a lot of time driving around looking for free parking, because I don't think it exists in this area. $3.00 an hour.   It's great if you live or work nearby and have the parking taken care of or are using transit to get here. Otherwise, if you're driving , forget it, unless have a meeting set up here. Work for a few hours and it's going to cost you, not to mention the inconvenience of having to go plug the meter. It's at the bottom of an apartment building, so I'm sure the residents know this location well.  Busyness - There are a lot of apartments around here so I expect this place is pretty busy in the morning. I wouldn't mind a Starbucks near my house.   Noisiness - For being pretty steady, it hasn't been too bad for a Friday afternoon. It's winter now, so I don't know what kind of crazy person would order an iced drink to get those blenders going. But, it is Canada.  Ambience - This isn't your usual boxy Starbucks. It clips off the bottom corner of the building so it has an unusual layout. The windows seem to line about half of the walls, so it's quite bright inside, with high ceilings.   Power - I didn't really see many outlets at the tables, so you may have a problem unless your laptop is miserly with the power.  Free wifi - Available and works well.  Staff - Friendly and efficient, as usual.  Seating - There are 21 seats here, with one large table to work at. If you're into relaxing in an easy chair, there are four of them tucked away in the corner and there's an electrical outlet there.  Perfect place for a power meeting or power nap. The rest of the tables are the square type with two chairs each. I like them much better than the useless round cafe style tables.  Security - It's a nice neighbourhood, but if you've read my other reviews you'll know I'm just paranoid about that kind of stuff.

text: You probably wonder what could possibly make the difference between one Starbucks from the next?   The difference is the employees. The manager, Dave, knows my name and remembers my order as do most of the employees there. The products are consistent with Starbucks, but these employees truly care about the product, which they all realize is not just coffee or espresso. These employees all recognize that the product is the service provided.   Dave, as a manager, built a team of who are hardworking, happy to be there, and take the time to get things right. Usually, the worst moments at a Starbucks are the confrontations between employee and disgruntled customer whose drink was made wrong. I've seen and heard employees make sure the drink they made was exactly how the customer wanted it. If it was wrong, the drinks were remade with a smile. There have been times where I've felt secondhand frustration from the situations unfolding, but the baristas worked hard and with a smile.   I've seen these employees cleaning constantly to ensure a positive atmosphere. I've also seen moments where the team as a whole worked so effectively and efficiently that they had a moment to converse and the topics were always lighthearted and filled with laughter, another sign of a cohesive team. The store engages the community as well, with things like a toy drive, clothing drive and other activities.   This Starbucks is what a Starbucks should strive for; a welcoming environment that ensures your product is exactly how you want it.

text: Why such a low rating?  Location, location, location.  Each knocks off one star.  It's a real shame that there are no cafes or coffee shops in Kennett square that are open past 2 on sunday afternoon: that's the only reason I headed out to this corporate chain situated in an ugly, suburban strip mall.  I feel dirty spending my money in a place like this, like I'm playing a role in wrecking the structure of American society.  That said, it really wasn't that bad.  Starbucks is starbucks.  I had a decent cup of tea and a tasty, but overpriced baked good.  That's par for the course.  Staff were friendly, and the atmosphere was tolerable.  I found the Christmas themed decorations a little bit lame, and this atmosphere is a bit colder and less cozy than some other Starbucks I've been in, but they're all more or less similar.  Get it together Kennett Square Merchants...you gotta have something open in the downtown every day of the week.  When I'm in town, I'll go there, guaranteed.  Maybe the Mexican bakery (which was open) could put some tables out; they sure have room for it in their large storefront:  http://www.yelp.com/biz/las-alondras-bakery-kennett-square  I'd rather give them my money than a Starbucks in some butt-ugly suburban shopping plaza that's just enriching some sleazy developer.

text: The atmosphere was typical Starbucks, the store was clean and lighting was nice.  The problem?  Me and my husband went to the store last week for coffee before our shopping expedition.  I ordered my regular drink; venti awake tea latte, sugar free hazelnut and 2 Splenda (A big sissy drink, I know).  We also ordered some pastries heated up.  The cashier was very friendly, we got our danishes and then off to the other end of the counter to pick up our drinks.    The barista was mixing up our drinks and flirting with a cute young boy standing by the counter.  I received my drink and tasted it to make sure that it was up to Parr.  It was missing all the delicious sugar free sugar.  I asked the barista if she added the sugar free hazelnut and Splenda.  She said she had and went back to flirting with the cute young stud.  I took another drink (I figured maybe my taste-buds were off).  Nope, definitely no sugary goodness. I asked her if she was sure, she said yes.  I asked her if she could add more sugar-free hazelnut.  With an exasperated sigh (as if I was interrupting her game), she did.  I opted to add my own Splenda so as not to disturb this budding relationship.    I am typically pretty easy to please.  I am not above fixing any taste problems with my drink myself.  I do, however work hard for my money and have no desire to spend it on something that is not given the same care that I give my customers.    This has got to be my least favorite Starbucks and I have gone to several all over northern NV and CA.  Also Southern CA.

text: Worst Starbucks experience, ever.  Even worse then stopping at a rest stop Starbucks - gasp.  I met a friend here on a Saturday morning, and it was chaos. We were getting on the road and wanted a quick pick-me-up before our long drive, but it would have been faster if I drove another shop and got it. I asked about it after I paid, and he confirmed I had ordered it but didn't get the coffee cake for me. I thought maybe they would hand it to me with the drink, but that did not happen. I ended up having to ask one of the other store personnel to get the coffee cake for me, after waiting for some time for my drink. Instead of letting us use the half-and-half, she disappeared with it to look for a carafe, which took more than 5 minutes before she found one and then filled it before we could finally use it.  There was no order in the store and it was just Mayhem. I don't think the staff is well trained and could definitely use more supervision or additional training. I don't think I'll stop by here again.

text: Had a really good customer service experience here that prompted me to write a review, and I noticed a fair amount of negative ones.  First, it is definitely not dirty.  Like most Starbucks, they clean incessantly.  Maybe the reviewer caught them right before some customer trashed the place.  The barista's are overall very nice.  Obviously, there may be a bad egg here or there, but, recently, I thought I ordered an iced coffee, but I was given and iced tea.  I'm pretty sure I was wrong and ordered an iced tea, but when I inquired, they didn't even think twice they promptly gave me an iced coffee, offered me to keep the iced tea, and gave me a coupon for a free drink on my next visit because my experience wasn't "perfect."  Pretty solid customer service to me.   As for the seating etc, I do wish the seating area was double the size and they had an entrance that was located on the side so the whole place doesn't get miserably cold in the winter with each opening.    As for the drinks, it's Starbuck's, so this one is par for the course.

I didn't know what to order until I leaned over my hubby & asked the friendly female voice coming thru where you order if they had a Hot Green Tea & she said "Yes"!  Then I asked what artificial sweeteners they had & she listed a few until I asked if they had Stevia & I was delighted to hear "Sure Do!"  Pulling up to the window, the friendly girl handed had my tea all ready, let me know she mixed in the 2 "Whole Earth" (Stevia w/monk fruit sweetener) and also gave me extra on the side in case I wanted more & my large straw!    5 Star Amazing Starbucks Experience & a Super Yummy Healthy Tea for me made perfectly!!!  I highly recommend this Starbucks & My order!!!

text: Maybe it was just an off day, or maybe it was because of training new people, but this was kind of a confusing Starbucks experience. I guess I might be spoiled, because most Starbucks locations really get the concept of the "customer experience" right.   A quick stop in for a nice chilly frappuccino on this hot afternoon started out a little bit strange when I was the next customer in line, and an employee standing at an empty till caught my eye and smiled brightly. I started to walk toward her and give her my order, and the employee at the other till, who had been helping the customer in front of me, broke off to reprimand me for trying to cut in front of that customer! I felt quite taken aback at the intensity of his response, to what was obviously a misunderstanding. I could barely even get out the words, "i'm so sorry, I thought she was asking me to come up and order."   When it was my turn, for real and not for pretend, it became apparent that she was a brand-new employee, being trained, and thus it was no surprise that it was a slightly confusing process to get my card reloaded, my items run in, and finally paid for (no big deal in itself; we have all been learners at something, at some point in our lives). Perhaps this brand-new employee also learned that it sends a mixed message to customers waiting in line when you make eye contact and smile at them, but you are totally not ready for them to actually come up and order...  However, when I picked up my drink, it had not been marked correctly, so it wasn't quite right. However, it was close, so I just took it and left; I clearly needed to consume some of that caffeine, before trying to wrap my brain around any more confusion ;)

The staff is also really friendly.  I end up joking with one of the guys while I place my order.  He usually crakes a joke at me as I walk off without receiving my muffin; I forget that I ordered one. The Jasper Ave location can get pretty busy so if you want to sit and study you need to come early in the morning or after the lunch rush.  The place is pretty busy but it's clean, the staff does a great job keeping up with the cleaning and reloading of sleeves, lids and flavored powders.  If you are the type of person whom can't study with too much noise then bring earphones and study to your own music to drown out the sounds of people talking.
