text: You would think that this Starbucks would be awesome because they are usually busy, have great local and tourist customers, and there are several other locations in a 3 block radius. WRONG. This Starbucks is THE worst Starbucks I have EVER been to. I have worked on State Street for years and every time I give them a chance, they always let me down. I used to only drink Starbucks coffee, but this location has made me learn to love Coffee Bean, which is right across the street.  Here are the worst parts of the service they provide (all of these things happen 90% of the time, no joke): 1. They act like taking your order is a nuisance. They roll their eyes and sigh if you take longer than 2 seconds to order. 2. They take FOREVER to make your order. I'm not talking 3, 5, or 7 minutes. They literally take 20 mins to make you a drink. It is because they don't care. I really think they hate making coffee here. i have seen them, with a super long line, have 2 people taking orders, 1 person doing nothing, and 1 person making all drinks. They do not care if you have somewhere to be, if you are on a break, if they are busy, or anything else about your life. They move only at 1 speed: DGAF. 3. The reason I write my review: Today, I went to get a coffee on my break. I was too lazy to walk the few extra feet to a different Starbucks or CBTL. I always regret when I do this. I gave my order and endured the normal attitude and eye rolling that is expected. I waited for the baristas to continue their personal conversations with their friends seated at the bar. When my 10 minute iced coffee was finally ready, the girl did not even break eye contact, let alone talking, set my coffee out and THREW my straw at me. THREW it. like tossed it so that it fell to the edge of the table. She didn't say my name. She didn't say my order. The signal to grab my drink wasn't a smile or eye contact. It was a thrown straw. I left disgusted that I just paid $3.95 to be insulted!! I know that it's just coffee, but this day in age, when times are "tough", the one luxury we all have is to spend our money where we choose. When something is so prevalent and there are so many options, stores should not rest on the fact that people "need" what they have. Because the truth is, we don't. I gave this Starbucks 3 years and probably close to 100 visits to earn my business and today it was literally a straw that broke the camel's back.  DON"T GO HERE.

text: Kudos to the baristas on my last visit. Still squinting against the cosmic rays of COVID-19's corona, they've opened the doors, but only to the extent of the long arm of the law: one giant step from the entryway to the interior's antisocial barrier. Waiting for service at a nearby car dealership whose coffee maker was removed against fear of the virus sent me over the hill through invisible woods to grab a cuppa. The Buck's brew is more to my liking, anyway.  The barista's voice smiled from beneath the facemask as she retrieved my mobile order. The drive-through lane was (surprisingly) empty and no one allowed inside, so service was immediate. The green to-go stopper (so often out-of-stock in busier pasts) had been pre-inserted by a magical, plasticized hand. Condiments are also banned to public fingers, so you'll need to ask specifically for anything you'd normally manage on your own at the walkup counter. Yet, a cheery and successful visit was had by all.  Your mileage will vary. Like its sibling locations, service varies with each crew and visit. It's still a Starbucks cookie-cutter, chain store experience. But we must all do our part to keep the beans brewing. Mischief managed.

Not wanting either of us to waste it, he suggested that I "bless someone who would appreciate it" and I managed to do just that. There tend to be homeless people hanging around across the street at my favorite place to eat. So, I  went across the street and approached a man with a sign and asked him if he likes iced coffee. He said he loved iced coffee and was more than willing to drink it. I gave it to him. I felt much better knowing that it wasn't going to waste, because, well, wasting Starbucks is just wrong. I also feel good knowing that we did something good for someone in need. This was a good trip!

text: I typically wouldn't spend the time writing a review of a Starbucks, to me they're all pretty much the same, however I want to give a big shoutout to the 1500 Market team for always getting my day off to a good start.   This location is a zoo, I mean suits and ties, pumps and pencil skirts packed to the gills all day every day. The line can be 15 deep and yet I have never waited from the end of the line to drink-in-hand more than 5 minutes. Now mind you I'm not exactly the "Venti 1 pump caramel, 1 pump white mocha, 2 scoops vanilla bean powder, extra ice frappuchino with 2 shots poured over the top (apagotto style) with caramel drizzle under and on top of the whipped cream, double cupped" kind, a simple iced coffee will do, however this place runs like a well oiled machine.  Line getting a little long? Don't sweat it, there's always a friendly faced employee taking advanced orders for drinks to expedite your wait time. By the time you get to the register to pay for your concoction, you're never waiting more than another minute or two for the barista to shout out your name.   Thanks 15th and Market S-Bucks for making my mornings a little brighter and still managing to get me to work in time.

text: HORRIBLE SERVICE!!! LACK OF COMMUNICATION!  I went to this location today (the day after Christmas) and was surprised that there was only one person in line as this location is usually always busy. I waited patiently as the woman in front of me was asking a bunch of questions about the syrups and sugar free items. I glanced at the board and noticed a huge sign advertising a FREE drink when you sign up on the Starbucks rewards app and register your card. I waited until the woman in front of me finished her order and when it was my turn I asked the cashier about the advertisement. I asked, "If I sign up right now, will I receive a free drink?" The cashier acted as if she didn't know what I was talking about so I pointed at the sign behind her and stated my question again. Again, she had a puzzled look on her face, then another worker chimed in and stated that I would receive an email of some sort with a coupon. I said, "Okay, I'll step aside and register now." As I sat in a chair right by the register I noticed a young woman and a homeless man. They were talking to one another, and to quickly sum up their conversation, she was in there to buy him (the homeless man) a drink and a sandwich; which I thought was a great gesture for someone in need while most people are celebrating the holidays. The young woman ordered the drink and food and I hear her tell the man that everything is paid for and that the worker would call him when it's done. She left the store after that. I'm pretty much done registering my card on the app and waiting for an email with my coupon. As I wait, one of the workers yells a woman's name and is holding a food bag. I just observe for the moment and say to myself "that was clearly the girl's who bought the sandwich for the homeless man." I began to feel a little antsy because it started getting crowded with people and I still had no email. I then hear the homeless man (he was speaking loudly every time he spoke) he's asking a cashier about his drink and food. Mind you, there are now two new cashiers at the register. I see the situation escalating because the cashier explains that he did not ring the man up and he would have to wait a second (as he was ringing another customer). The homeless man just says "what do you mean? It's already been paid for. The girl, the girl bought it for me! a hot chocolate and a sandwich." I no longer could just stand by. I got up from the chair and I asked the male cashier where the female cashier went who was just on the registers. I said "I saw everything, a young woman was in here and she bought him food and left!" and as I'm explaining this the female cashier peeks her head around the corner and I point stating "she rung her up" (As I did this, I see the food and sandwich sitting off to the side together, unclaimed). I was just fed up, I yelled "There's the food right there! What is the problem? Why are y'all not communicating? This is horrible service, I'm leaving". So I left, with no drink and no further explanation of this supposedly FREE drink they were advertising. I hope that man was able to get his drink and sandwich, he clearly needed it. It's really a shame that the young woman took the time to help him, trying to do a good deed for someone, and the workers there just totally ruined everything. I will never return to this location, it's not the first time I've had a bad experience.

text: The McMullen Booth Starbucks is my primetime chill out spots. It's one of the most spacious and least crowded that I've been to, which is why it is close to heaven for readers.  The interior is decorated in the rich, warm colors that Starbucks is known for, but unlike most of the cafes this one has two long, wooden tables for multiple patrons to sit and enjoy their java. This is perfect for groups, and personally my husband & I love coming here as a date night for some drinks and board games.  If you're looking for comfort, they also have leather chairs that are soft enough to sink into and stay for a while. When I'm not bringing the hubby for date night, you can find me with a kindle in one hand and a tall caramel macchiato in the other(which I order with nonfat milk to make it less than 200 calories. Yum!).  The onnnnnly gripe I have, is that sometimes the sounds of their appliances can be quite noisy, but who's ever heard of a quiet blender?   All in all, this is a wonderful place to come sip a tasty drink and kick back. The environment is calm & cool, the baristas are quirky & perky, and of course the drinks are always delish.

text: I was going to preface this review with a defense of Starbucks, but changed my mind and decided to not get into the politics and simply stick to reviewing.  (Though I will say - politics does influence where I will spend my money.)  In some ways, a Starbucks is a Starbucks.  At least if you are at a Company Store and not a Licensed Location (of which there are thankfully few.) However, in the world of "the same" this location stands out as wonderful.  The Baristas are uniquely friendly.  They also do seem to try harder to be helpful than at other locations.  I have on a few occasions needed to speak to a manager, not for problems, but to get information, and the woman who seems to be the store manager is knowledgeable and responsive.  She also seems to have a good rapport with the staff, which may account for the quality of service.  The store, and specifically the restroom, is always clean, and when I have found it lacking in a paper product, they are quick to fix the problem.  Although the lines can be long, service doesn't dawdle.  On the other hand, when it isn't busy, staff is happy to chat about coffee, or movies, if you are interested and don't interfere with their work getting done.  There are plenty of tables, and four areas which have power outlets, which is more than most locations, so it is truly possible to be there and get work done.  It does get crowded at times, especially during exam weeks at Drexel and Penn, but I have only once, out of many visits had to leave because I couldn't find a seat.  This is not the ONLY Starbucks in Philly that I like, but it is my favorite!   On a Company-wide note - they now use NO High Fructose Corn Syrup in ANYTHING.  Desserts are back on my menu!

text: Best Starbucks in Tampa! The manager here, Dena,  is absolutely amazing! Very cheery, always willing to assist staff as well. They take great pride in their Starbucks and it shows! text: UPDATE: Omg. I'm just done with this Starbucks until they figure out that whatever they are doing is NOT working. I get it that it's in a busy location on a Main Street but all the more reason to make sure that things are running efficiently. The lines are forever long and sometimes the people on the registers are sooooooo freaking slow! Beyond slow. The other day I stood in line for more than 10 minutes...I was third in line..! It's not like the line was out the door...I was third. Then it took forever to get my drink...I drink black iced coffee...that's it.  It just seems ridiculous to me that this store can't seem to get with it. It's a great store...the location is picturesque and perfect, it's comfortable and spacious, but if your in a hurry then it's not a good place to be.  I live around the corner but I get in my car and drive 5 miles to the location in flourtown because they are quick and friendly and their employees always seem happy to be there.  The chestnut hill location just needs an overhaul badly.   Also....think that mobile ordering would help this situation..!!!? NOPE! You can mobile order, then come in and find a place to wait and stand in the way while you wait and wait for your mobile order to be done. OR You can wait a bit until it might be ready but then come in and find that because they didn't mark things not available in their system they don't have a single thing that u ordered. So now you have to re pick all your stuff based on what they do have. I've had to do that the last two times I mobile ordered which is supposed to save time. It doesn't at this location.  I think I'll keep going to flourtown for a while and maybe during that time they will figure things out in chestnut hill.

text: Your ordinary Starbucks location nestled within a rest stop, so there really isn't any fanfare that this Starbucks would be better than in any ordinary city (such as nearby Philadelphia), or even a local mall. No seating or stools, but then again, most likely you've arrived by car (or motorbike), or on a bus for a wait, so you certainly won't stay longer than you would at any other Starbucks enjoying a relaxing cup of coffee and a book/eBook/laptop. Most of their products are fairly consistent with other Starbucks offerings, so it might be a relief.  The line starts near the main entrance of the rest stop, not at the side entrance, to avoid confusion, and you receive your coffee/beverage at the left. The amenity station offers your standard fare of half and half milk, whole milk, sugars (natural, refined, alternative).   My experience there wasn't out of the ordinary, but since the oven was inoperable during my visit, my double chocolate brownie was slightly warm, but still great to enjoy, and the employees seemed cheery, even in a place such as a rest stop.  Overall, if you enjoy coffee, and Starbucks, you would be in luck to come here on the way to your destination on a road trip / work commute.

text: A while ago, it was a very rare event when I went to a Starbucks drive-through. That changed when Starbucks moved into the old Burger King building near my house. While the service is always prompt and friendly despite the long lines, the quality of what they make can be spotty.  I'm a big lover of matcha green tea lattes, and all kinds of brewed drinks. a big part of what I love about brewed drinks is the distinct flavor of what is being brewed. So when I come here, and I order a matcha green tea latte, or any other selection, and I get a cup of warm milk with only a splash of what I actually ordered, it grinds my gears. I get that some of the only reasons people drink Starbucks is to show off to the world that they're hip and cultured, but some people actually like the flavor of what's being brewed. I could easily warm up some milk on the stove, put it in a paper coffee cup, and lug it around with me, but that isn't what I'm playing for.  If this location fails to quit serving warm milk, I'll have to start going to another location, or coffee place all together. These establishments are far too costly to only serve warm milk.

text: Like most Yelpers have already mentioned, Starbucks is going to be Starbucks. Sometimes, you'll have an exceptional experience, but more often than not, you'll just be here to grab what you need and leave. The Starbucks inside this Sheraton on Canal Street is no different, but luckily this hotel actually has a Starbucks.  Recently, I stayed at the Sheraton for a conference and was annoyed by the fact that we weren't offered breakfast. Luckily, this Starbucks saved my life. Open very early at around 5am, you can definitely turn to this Starbucks for your snack needs. This Starbucks has your usual coffee, bananas, desserts, breakfast items, as well as water.  However, there's several cons worth mentioning at this Starbucks. For one, the space is small. On high traffic days, you'll be waiting a while just to order and you'll be lucky to find a seat. Also, I'm not even sure there's Starbucks WI-FI, but at least if you have a room, Sheraton WI-FI reaches you here?  But the worst thing is that prices for their items are ridiculous. Not sure if it's hotel surge pricing or competitive corporate pricing, but I was so annoyed at the fact that I had to pay $6 for a stupid parfait and $6 for a fruit cup. That's crazy expensive! But at least Starbucks is here if you do need food (shrugs)?  Overall, I am grateful that the hotel has a Starbucks. It offered me water, food, and a place to relax when I needed it. But prices are so high, the space is small, and there's no WI-FI! I give this place a "tolerable, sufficient for sustenance" rating.  Eat well and prosper,

text: As far as Starbuck's go, this one is definitely middle of the road.  They do have some outside seating which is nice but inside is nothing to write home about.  Standard run of the mill furniture with a lot of small tables.  Lots and lots of white.  Definitely could use a bit more color and decoration to liven it up a bit.  Staff are friendly and attentive but the lines drag a bit in the morning.  Not a place you can typically get a regular coffee at say 8 AM and be out the door in under 2-3 minutes.  Looking at least at a 5-10 minute wait.      It greats crowded on the weekends too and finding a seat inside is pretty tough by Noon.  Lots of students studying and to a lesser degree people working.  What is nice is that you have a bit more space than other Starbucks which is good if you need to spread out a bit to work.       It's convenient for me because it is right around the corner but parking can be a bit of a drag at this location too.  If you need a Starbuck's fix or want to get here early enough to grab a seat on the weekend, this location works but if I wanted to enjoy a coffee I would probably walk up to one of the places in downtown Clayton instead.

text: This place used to be amazing and I LOVED coming here, but lately it has gone down hill. And when I say down hill, I mean DOWN HILL. It absolutely SUCKS now.   Curious as to why? Please read below:   Wait Time: The wait is always forever. I feel like I could knit myself a sweater in the time it takes to go through that drive through and that's if I'm the only car in the drive through. I completely understand that Starbucks is popular and very busy, especially during peak times, but why is it that management cannot staff to demand? Is it really to difficult to know that during peak hours, the establishment might need a few extra hands? Now, I know the reasonable thing many might be thinking: Mobile Ordering. An absolutely fantastic idea. And for a while, worked wonderfully. Unfortunately, it seems as though it is impossible for these orders to be made correctly. Not sure why mobile orders are any different from ordering at the drive through or in store, but there's something that just makes them impossible to be made correctly. Also, in case anyone is curious, I haven't ordered anything complicated, especially through mobile ordering. If I want non-fat milk or sugar free substitutes, I order at the Starbucks.   Cups: Most recently, my sister and I came in and ordered some frappes and we received very liquid and disgusting drinks in sticky cups. Now I know I shouldn't have high expectations, but I really do not think expecting a clean cup is too much to ask for.  What really put this trip over the edge was that when I asked for new cups, the barista ignored the fact that they gave me sticky, dripping cups and just put my drink (in the same cup) into another cup. So I walked out with my drink in literally two cups. Now, I know it technically solved the problem, but this is not the first time this has happened. In fact, I cannot remember the last time I received a drink from this Starbucks and the cup was not dripping or sticky. The absolute worst is when you stop before work, in your work clothes, and you don't notice the liquid on the outside of the cup and it drips all over your clothes. Sorry Starbucks, didn't realize I needed to watch out for part of my drink on the outside of my cup. Is it too much to ask for my drink not to be on the outside its holder?  Quality: I would also like to mention the quality of the drinks at this Starbucks. Now, I'm not a connoisseur of coffee. I do not have high standards when it comes to the quality and taste. As long as it tastes like it's supposed to - I'm good. That being said, I cannot remember the last time my drink from this Starbucks was actually right. It's been close, it's been drinkable, but it has absolutely not been correct/perfect, or what it is supposed to taste like. One might then ask, "Well how do you know what it's supposed to taste like?" My response: "I've been drinking Starbs for years - it's what got me through college. There's no way they changed their recipes for all my favorite drinks in the past few months." It's honestly just sad. I'm not a barista nor do I pretend to know the trials and tribulations of what it might be to be one, I'm sure it's difficult and my respect to all the amazing baristas out there, BUT don't many of your barista instruments have measurement marks on them? Doesn't Starbs use specific measurements for many, if not all, their drinks? Again, I'm not a barista, but it just seems like it would be really difficult to use the incorrect ingredient measurements. I'm sure if someone was new, of course it would be difficult to remember all the measurements and what goes in what drink, but come on. Eventually, after making so many drinks, it would have to become second nature or at least somewhat of a habit. Also, the amount of Starbucks trips that have resulted in a cup of milk being handed to me has been one to many. At this point, we must have to realize that there's something wrong in the coffee making process. I would never go to Starbucks and pay $5 for a cup of steamed milk. Overall: I'm just really disappointed in this specific Starbucks location. When I say it got me through college, it literally got me through college. I loved coming to Starbucks. It was my treat for the day and it just made my day better. That being said, I know it's silly to base my entire day on how my cup of coffee was, but is it really too much to go to an establishment which specializes in a product and expect their product to meet a certain set of expectations? The purpose of this review is not to hurt anyone or be rude, it's to help someone in charge realize that there is a problem and there are many coffee drinkers who want this problem to be resolved. It just makes me sad how down hill this place has gone.  I really hope this review makes it to someone who can do something to help this caterpillar of a store because I KNOW it has the potential to be a beautiful butterfly. Until then, I guess I'm reaping the benefits of saving $5/day.

doesn't starbs use specific measurements for many, if not all, their drinks? again, i'm not a barista, but it just seems like it would be really difficult to use the incorrect ingredient measurements. i'm sure if someone was new, of course it would be difficult to remember all the measurements and what goes in what drink, but come on. eventually, after making so many drinks, it would have to become second nature or at least somewhat of a habit. also, the amount of starbucks trips that have resulted in a cup of milk being handed to me has been one to many. at this point, we must have to realize that there's something wrong in the coffee making process. i would never go to starbucks and pay $5 for a cup of steamed milk. overall: i'm just really disappointed in this specific starbucks location. when i say it got me through college, it literally got me through college. i loved coming to starbucks. it was my treat for the day and it just made my day better. that being said, i know it's silly to base my entire day on how my cup of coffee was, but is it really too much to go to an establishment which specializes in a product and expect their product to meet a certain set of expectations? the purpose of this review is not to hurt anyone or be rude, it's to help someone in charge realize that there is a problem and there are many coffee drinkers who want this problem to be resolved. it just makes me sad how down hill this place has gone.  i really hope this review makes it to someone who can do something to help this caterpillar of a store because i know it has the potential to be a beautiful butterfly. until then, i guess i'm reaping the benefits of saving $5/day.

As far as waiting time in store or drive thru, nothing change even worse now. As far as cleanse, garbage always full..bar has nasty look and no one bother to wipe it off. According to health code in the city, you can't put all powder continer exposed in the front counter on the reach of the costumers.  I decided to stop spending my $20 daily at Starbucks and buy a professional maker and do it my self.  I think the people who left 5 stars reviews are one time visitors. God Bless America, this store will be reported to the HQ

text: It's Starbucks...mostly consistent factory product delivery with variation in the accessibility, architecture, staff and local management. This is a relatively new spot (so new that Google maps doesn't fully know about it as of this post.) Kudos to the team as staff are pleasant and on point. Your mileage is going to vary as "disappointing" is the best I can say overall, though you can still satisfy your buzz crave.  Accessibility gets low marks due to poor design of the strip center near the Ovation property that may or may not ever fully materialize. Parking is a challenge and will only become worse as the area expands to more fully suck. Lanes are narrow, few, and full even during off-peak hours.  Architecture and design earn fails for both the garage band harshness of what should feel like an invitation to someone's bonus room and lack of convenient access to amenities. Decor is comfy and open, but noise reduction is either missing altogether or substantially neglected by the designers. Forget conversation, but no one talks these days. Right?  Convenience also seems to have missed the design requirements. Customers must swim 10-15 yards upstream from the door through the line or circumnavigate the tables into the rear of the space to order. Add to that a lack of trash cans; none may be found in view if they actually exist in the general customer space at all. Two small (not much larger than a Grande cup) orifices exist built-in at the pickup counter. Wade through all waiting customers (as well as those grazing over condiments and napkins) and you'll arrive at the too-small trash holes whose bins are usually filled to the wood of the counter top. It scores two thumbs way, way down as they help your fingers cram refuse into a wet lack of space.

text: The best part about this particular Starbuck's location is the interior décor. It's a nice place to sit and talk with whomever you brought to this location, but that's about it. If you want to sit and hang out at this location, unfortunately and I mean unfortunately, you will probably have to buy something from them.  I think that Starbuck's coffee isn't really all that good. I heard that there was this new location, so I wanted to try it out. I didn't think that I would get great coffee because I have never gotten great coffee from any Starbuck's location. Today was no different. I ordered a Vanilla Bean Crème Frappacino and got a cold drink that tasted like vanilla ice cream. If I wanted vanilla ice cream, I would have gotten the carton out of my freezer and eaten it. The bottled frappicinos that you can buy in the convenience stores and grocery stores are so much better tasting than what I bought.  The customer service was from somewhat lackadaisical employees, one of whom just acted like he didn't understand anything that was said to him.   Some people may think that "it's cool to hate Starbuck's," but when they consistently have less than stellar customer service and their products don't taste good, that's not being cool to hate Starbuck's; those are valid reasons not to like Starbuck's and this location hasn't given me a reason to like their coffee very much either. Overall, I think that Starbuck's is overrated and overpriced for what they have.  I would much rather patronize the small independent coffee shops around town than to patronize a corporate establishment because the small independent coffee shops need the business more that the large corporate coffee chains need it. The small independent coffee shops always seem to have better coffee and infinitely better customer service, too.

text: As much as I try and support smaller coffee shops more often than not, I can't help but crave my standard Starbucks treat on busy days.  Generally the lines aren't too bad and I really have to respect the fact they are offering healthier options in the showcase. Some newer additions would include a protein plate and paninis. I believe availability differs from store to store. My top 2 drinks: A simple skinny vanilla latte half sweet with a triple shot of espresso. Gets the job done.  And more often lately- a skinny chai latte. I usually get it a bit on the stronger side. It has a good kick of spice and as one of the Baristas told me pretty healthy for you since it's mainly real tea with a small amount of sugar added to the mix. It's more of a subtle caffeine fix than the usual coffee fix and doesn't give u the typical coffee breath. Their vivanno smoothies are really good as well. They are made with real banana and whey protein. And the instant flavored coffee packs available are really a good investment. Super convenient when on the go, and good paired with cold water or steamed hot milk.

text: I can honestly say that this is my favorite Starbucks in Nashville, if not, my favorite late-night coffee shop in town. While most shops close at 10 or 11pm, are dimly lit, have poor Wi-Fi capability, and blare loud music, this one is definitely a work-conducive environment. I come here quite a bit just to get work done late at night, and am always happy with my productivity level.  On a downside, due to the location, it may be difficult to find parking at times (note: the neighboring back parking lot seems to be fine passed 9pm, when the other businesses close). As far as seating goes, I've rarely had issues finding a place to sit. The one time I did, I plopped myself right in front of the fireplace outside, and basked in the fiery heat. It was pure awesomeness.  The bathroom, however, could use a little more upkeep. Tonight, I went in twice in the span of a few hours, and both times, the toilet paper roll was merely sitting atop the dispenser, not inside of it. I tried not to think of all the hands that grazed the toilet paper roll... (sigh). But at least the soap and paper towels were well stocked in order to have some level of sanitation.   Other than that, the staff were friendly, helpful, and extremely service-oriented in getting your order to you as quick as possible. So far in the six months I've been in town, in my opinion, this is Nashville's best late-night work spot.

text: I really gave this place a few shots before posting this review because Starbucks are busy, I get it. But I have had a few instances in the past week that I've never seen at another Starbucks in all of my years of buying their coffee.  Earlier this week, I put in a mobile order and arrived about 10 minutes later when the estimated time was 4-7 minutes. I got there and there was one man in line, and two girls behind the counter. The one was working on someone else's drink (maybe another mobile order I assumed). Turned out, it was the man who had only just ordered. I always understood mobile orders to be your place in line, but I didn't say anything. I waited about 8 minutes for my whole order (I got multiple drinks but the longest thing I waited on was food because one of the girls behind the counter was stocking lids very slowly instead of making anything). The next day, I put my mobile in earlier expecting the wait to be longer as it was a Monday. Put it through about 15 minutes early, still the whole order was not ready when I arrived! Took at least another 10 minutes for them to realize that they never finished one drink.  Then this morning, I put in my mobile order in early. It was a Saturday at like 630 am, so in my mind, it would be more than enough time. I arrived and stood around because the drink wasn't ready yet. Found it odd because as I was standing there the workers didn't say anything. Finally, I asked if my drink was waiting behind the counter or something. This is when someone finally informed me that the espresso machine had not been cleaned, so she couldn't actually make my drink as the machine wasn't working. I was trying to catch a train, had stood there for several minutes before I even said anything, and at the very least the employee could've reimbursed me on a gift card and left that out to let me know something wasn't right. I will not go back to this location. In all honesty, it's never busy, which I have to assume is because so many others before me have come to the same conclusion.

text: I used to go to this Starbucks all the time when I was in grad school. Service was always fine. However, this last time, we stopped in the morning after Thanksgiving on our way out of town. We seriously waited 3-4 minutes in the store before anyone acknowledged us verbally. It was pretty ridiculous. There were 7 or 8 people waiting and finally, once the guy making drinks made a move to talk to us, the woman working the counter finally just stared at us at the register until we ordered. Really? Then she made a big show out of writing our names on our cups when we were the only people waiting for drinks. Pretty surreal.   I do remember that people tend to camp out in this location and set up shop for quite a while. If you're meeting someone socially, it can be hard to find a table. In warmer months, there is ample outdoor seating, however.   The drive-through can be tricky as there are two entrances and there always seem to be issues with the two lanes merging.

text: The only reserve coffee Starbucks in the area.  Short wait but I would say they aren't the quickest.  (Try the municipal towers in Seattle and you'll see what I mean).  Store itself is comfortably laid out with nice back to back benches, one side street facing and the other facing the counter.  Large room off to the side with a large rectangular table in the center.  Cell charging stations under the table and some on the side of the walls   Only two bathrooms and code needed.  When I was there the code didn't work and they needed a key override.    One eye opener during my second visit is seeing a gal sit on one of the tables against the window.  The Germophobe  part of me realized that table tops are now not off limits for street germs.  Ugh.  Maybe it's a generational thing but I never place my shoes where I eat!!  All in all, I will overlook these things on search of the Clover experience.    Now if only Starbucks will offer mobile orders for all locations!

text: Yelp keeps suggesting that I review Starbucks, so here goes...  It's Starbucks...there's nothing that I can say that hasn't been said already.  Except for maybe this:  I despise coffee.   As my Mom used to say, it's an insult to the palate and an abomination to the stomach.    So, much to my surprise, they actually have a pretty decent tea selection.  Not that I'm an expert...I recently had to take up tea drinking after voluntarily giving up Mountain Dew after a 20 year bender.     Every once in a while, I have a friend who wants to meet at Starbucks and I grit my teeth and acquiesce.  I usually order the plain black tea.  And, to my astonishment, it's pretty freakin' good!  Better than the Lipton that I get out of the Keurig, which isn't half bad either.  The other day, I decided to live on the edge and order the Chai tea, black.  Holy s***...what have I been missing? Luckily the Starbucks Chai tea K-cups are available on Amazon for a reasonable price.   Back to Starbucks at the Park Place Mall...the employees are super friendly.  I'm literally the last person on the bandwagon, I know, but they really seem to go above and beyond.  So yeah, go there...like you don't already know.

text: Although I try to stick with locally owned coffee houses, this is probably my favorite Starbucks in Reno. It isn't in the best location. It is not surrounded by a good view; it's right on the side of Greg Street in a rather run down, little shopping area. But, for me, this location is in a good spot. I take the route along this area often and since this Starbucks has a drive-thru, I can just swing in and get a cup of coffee or one of their other infamous beverages. (My favorite is their Passionfruit Shaken Iced Tea Lemonade! Yum!) Every single time I have been here I have been satisfied with the customer service. The staff is polite, professional and they run the drive-thru well. (I have never actually stayed for coffee here. I think there are many other more pleasant Starbucks locations, in Reno, if you're wanting to kick back and sip on something tasty.) They are fairly quick, with getting you your order, much quicker than anytime I have actually been inside of a Starbucks. Even when there are a few cars, in front of me, I've never had to wait more than about ten minutes. The drinks have always been mixed-well and held up the taste you expect, from Starbucks. As far at the drive-thru goes, they are A-OK!

text: I'm not sure what happened to this location in the last 3 months.  The purpose of a mobile order is to order ahead to bypass the line or drive-thru to avoid the wait while being sure to allow enough time for the "Order Ready" estimate, run in, grab-and-go.  It's for people who want their coffee or breakfast, but know that they won't have time for point-of-service ordering.  If I had the time to wait the additional 10-15 minutes for my order to be ready after allowing for the estimated drive/prep time, then I wouldn't have used their app in the first place.  My experience the last 3 visits with ordering ahead 5 minutes before leaving and the approximate 8 minute drive from home averages a 10 minute wait.  Fifteen minutes one additional morning, but they were very busy on this particular Friday morning at 7am, so I was patient because I understood.  Today, I placed a breakfast order ahead of time in a time crunch (2 sandwiches, no java), arrived 5 minutes after it was placed (with a 6 minute order ready estimate, so I did expect a slight wait if they were busy), waited 10 minutes for them to finally tell me that one of the items I ordered was not available.  Game over, time wasted, running late because of this now.....again.  This time though, there were only 2 cars in the drive-thru, no one at the counter ordering, one person waiting for one coffee, and 5 employees working. text: I'm really glad this Starbucks opened up on Harbour Island, long overdue! We can now have coffees and teas and custom beverages at standard Starbucks prices and right near American social and Jackson's!   It's a pretty standard Starbucks with points available for collection (not redemption), same high quality beverages and usually carries the seasonal specials. One of the great things about this Starbucks over some of the others in South Tampa is this one isn't that busy and it has ample electrical outlets available. Almost every seat has charging. It is well kept, staff are friendly and seating is comfortable.   There is however negatives too- you can park in the paid parking, and get your parking validated here (for free), but that makes parking a little inconvenient. It also closes a little early for a regular Starbucks (read 8pm). The biggest negative is they don't allow you to use Starbucks reward points. You can collect them but can not redeem them here. So it kind of eliminates the use of points if you only come here for your Starbucks.

text: I often go to this Starbucks because it is closest to my home.  The service is hit or miss probably because they seem to have new trainees here every time I am here.  The long time (e.g., more than a year) employees are usually very friendly and accurately make my order.  The problem is the trainees are often clueless especially when I redeem a reward and have to ask for advice from a more experienced employee. This tends to slow down the ordering process and results in overcharges.  I often have to repeat my order several times because many of the barista trainees  have short term memory issues which is sad because they are so young.  Given the high customer traffic at this location I think Starbucks should not staff this store with so many trainees. I gave this location two stars mainly because of the good service provided by the few, and ever diminishing, experienced employees; if I were to base this on the trainees, I would give no stars.

They are getting too big and too busy and service is suffering. At least train your staff to deal with customers properly. text: A decent spot to grab a cup of coffee or some food before going through the day. This Starbucks is part of the University of the Sciences and will host a plethora of students. You can use your declining balance or Devils dollars here. A great spot to get quick tasty breakfast. Literally the best option for food in general on this campus that doesn't cost you real money.   For non students: This location is nothing compared to a non university affiliated starbucks, but  you can always get the same options you can at other locations. They will take cash and card and treat you no different. There are usually many students here who will either be studying or eating, so seating can be tricky. Definitely a go to when you visit the school.   Phone wi-fi does not work here for some reason. There just doesn't seem to be service and nothing ever connects. Laptops do work here however.   This is a pretty nice locstion and typical location for a University. Better food here than most places and a nice atmosphere.

Beware, though the lines are long.  The first morning I went I think I waited almost 10 minutes to order my drink and then nearly another 10 to get my drink.  They were working fast, there was just a lot of people ordering drinks and food.  I cut down on some of that the next day by using mobile order and pay, so I'd order when I left the hotel, but even still I had to wait a few minutes once I got there for my drink.  It wasn't a huge deal, and once I knew I planned for it, but the lines were long every morning I went, so be prepared.

text: I'll be completely honest. I'm not a Starbucks fan or even a coffee drinker so my perspective may be slightly skewed towards the unfavorable but I will say that even if I was a huge coffee drinker and fan of Starbucks, this particular location seemed especially sad to me. And here's why.  Take out what you'd normally get at a Starbucks: coffee and your name spelled wrong on the coffee cup by the hipster working behind the register, and what you're left with is the atmosphere and just how the store feels when you're there.  Every time I go to this store to pick something up for my friends, it felt dead. I mean there were people there but it just didn't feel alive. The soft music playing in the background was more depressing than not, the tables and chairs weren't inviting and felt unwanted, even though it wasn't sunny outside, they'd have their curtains drawn halfway which made it even darker inside than it should be. Also every time I go there and there're people sitting at the tables, they'd be speaking softly to each other in a somber mood. I don't know. I just don't like the vibe at this particular spot. I used to work at Starbucks way back in my college days and it was an exciting spot with streams of people, loud music, bright lights, etc. It was inviting and upbeat. This place, I feel like every time I go here I'm intruding in on some funeral.  Alas, this is the closest Starbucks from my home and my friends leech off of me so because I'm so nice (maybe not in this review) I'll probably keep coming here for their drinks.  Parking: Shared lot Price: What you'd normally pay at a Starbucks Wait: None

text: This Starbucks location is a franchisee located in the Bridgeton Target. I frequent this Starbucks when I am on a Target run and unfortunately it's the only one located on this long busy business strip.  There is an obvious difference between this franchisee Starbucks and other company owned Starbucks. They never have all the same pastries, wraps and salads that you'll find at a company owned Starbucks. They also receive limited quantities for seasonal drink additives so when they run out, they run out and they aren't able to restock.  Getting a consistent drink here is really hit or miss. I no longer have to state how many pumps of toffee-nut or mocha I would like in my drink (keep in mind you never have to do that at a company owned store because they are trained on proper usage and only deviate per customer requests). This location has lots of the regular Starbucks swag and seasonal coffee. Now you can get the 10-cent discount on your drink purchase when you bring in a travel coffee mug.  All in all, in a pinch and needing a legal addictive stimulant - this place will do.

text: This Starbucks is in a good location. The problem is the SLOW service! When you walk in, whether there is 1 person ahead of you in line or 10 people, you can expect it will be 15-20 minutes to grab a simple drink. I don't know if it's poor staffing or something else, but it's insane. I used to go to this Starbucks every day before work, and each time I was late for work. I finally gave up. There always looks like there's 3-4 people working in the morning but it's still so slow, much slower than any other Starbucks. There seems to be a lack of concern for customer's time--no urgency. The staff seems friendly enough to the locals that they know and like...and they ignore the others. The staff usually talks amongst themselves which is frustrating when you're waiting for a drink and employees are babbling away together. It sends and unprofessional vibe.   Also, they only have one station for cream/sugar and everything is usually empty and messy. And the setup is ridiculous--where people wait for their drinks is also where you try to fix up your drink. It's a madhouse. They need to seriously redesign that aspect.   Bottom line: for overpriced coffee, try Chestnut Hill Coffeehouse a block further up Germantown. It's quicker and the coffee is better.

text: Not the most efficient Starbucks that I have ever been to.  We visited on a typical weekday evening to find that the place was pretty busy. I feel that when a place is super busy that is the easiest way to make sure that everyone gets their drink. I mean its Starbucks so its not like every single person is going to order something different.  The girl that took my order seemed to be a little frazzled by how busy they were. IDK how else to word it other than that. Just felt that something was a little off. Then you had several people actually making drinks. I counted 3...which you would think that would mean you'd get your drink faster. But that was not correct either. Two people seemed to be not only making drinks but also trying to manage the drive thru. They carried a personal conversation the whole time but talked loud enough that everyone could hear.  Despite the lack of organization the drinks did come out in the order in which they were ordered. That was impressive. Also with everyone kind of doing their own thing only 1 persons drink was wrong. Apparently a guy wanted his drink cold and they made it hot.  When my drink finally came out I felt that it wasn't made like the other places. Its not that hard to make a passion tea lemonade sweetened either...but apparently they did something wrong. Due to the slow pace and overall appearance that no one really knew what was going on...I did not ask them to re-make it. It wasn't horrible by any means...just not what I was expecting. Maybe they left the sweetener out?  Regardless I feel that I will pass up on this location and choose to give my business to another location...one that is more efficient and quicker.

text: When Starbucks first initiated its expansion it stressed service and quality for price. It was also known for providing good pay and benefits to its employees.   Because I believed in those positive service traits of a good business I invested by purchasing a few shares of the company stock.   Then Starbucks raised prices on all its drinks, ceased offering free internet connection at the stores, and declined in service to customers through longer wait time for orders, less attention to the cleanliness of the store, and workers not willing to listen to orders and requests of customers.   Most coffee shops in my area offer free internet service-and are willing to make coffee at 1/2 the price of Starbucks-Its A Grind in particular and McDonald's are more competitive in their price and service structure.   We sincerely hope Starbucks' management will work to deliver better service, value, and product once again to the clientèle it once served more professionally.

text: Decent Starbucks. Very small parking lot, maybe 5-6 spots. Behind it is a huge Lake & in the winter when it's frozen, it turns into an ice rink. Kids and adults alike, play hockey and twirl around from morning till sunset. They all pile into Starbucks to warm up after, so it's always busy during the winter. There's about 10-12 tables/chairs down one side of the store. There are four leather club chairs by the front window which are a coveted spot. Across the front there's a counter top with some stools. Two big, clean bathrooms in the back, with baby changing stations. The place was clean. My particular barista couldn't have been nicer! There's also additional seating out back, overlooking the scenic lake for the warmer months. The only thing missing is a fireplace. The Newtown location has one, which makes it my favorite location. text: Dear Starbucks,  Recently heard a quote that I thought was worthy of the general feel of this and the greater part of your stores.  "Just because you're the best at something doesn't mean you're actually good"-Doug Stanhope.  Well, unfortunately, this is not a time I relish saying "I told you so", but this place.  Gets worse by the day.  I've included my review from November 2014 a  few lines below, and well... I told you so.  Don't rely on my impressions alone.  Just peruse the recent reviews of this spot!  This location, currently, like all in NOLA, is just so subpar by any measure.  When under the original store manager, this place hummed.  It virtually operated on a raised vibrational plane.  Anyone who frequented a couple of years ago knows what Ima saying.  Was the fastest, friendliest, freshest, quality, expertly crafted Siren's snug you could happen upon.    Now?  A factory of lifeless, corporately churned sadness.  Nowadays NOLA's coffee-loving veins are coursing with the lifeblood of far too many superior 3rd waves to even consider this, this...ardently formulaic inferiority.   The only decent Starbucks in the area is the Kenner location on Esplanade, curiously NOT in the NOLA market.    MY ORIGINAL REVIEW FROM NOVEMBER 2014 FOLLOWS:  Although I travel a lot, I consider myself a regular at this location when I'm back home in Metry.  I experienced the old store down the street and all it's immense problems (parking, drive through, etc), and the move to this new location earlier this year.  Great improvements were made all across the board in the areas around: 1) quality of employees, 2) presentation of the store, 3) quality of the products, and most importantly, 4) quality of service and atmosphere.  Well, now.  I sensed something was amiss last week on my 1st visit since around the middle of October.  I went through the drive through and the service was uncharacteristically slow.  And I mean slow to the tune of 25 minutes in line with only 6-8 cars in line.  Had nearly the same experience a couple of days later, and the kicker?  The drinks were wrong, and nearly undrinkable, both times.  This from a location that I considered to be the best around for a proper drink.  So I go in the store a couple of days ago and ask a customer, with whom I've become acquainted, what was going on?  He replies that Jay is gone.    I don't go around offering reviews on personalities, etc., but the loss of Jay as the manager of this store is gonna be crippling.  This man worked tirelessly to get to know every single customer, knew all of our drinks, always asked if everything was perfect, he was a walking coffee encyclopedia, was always sweeping the floors, making sure everything was just right.  I can go on, but you get the picture.  Any of the regulars who knew Jay will know exactly what I mean.  I ask a barista, "Where's Jay?"  Strangely, the reply goes something like, "Don't really know what happened.  I think the management culture makes the store manager's job impossible."  And I'm told several baristas have quit or asked to be reassigned as a result.  Starbucks, I don't know what is going on here, and I don't know the entire story, but if you can't hold on to an employee the quality of Jay, you have serious institutional issues.  I have never met a more caring, resourceful, hard working person as the man you have apparently now lost.  His dedication to Starbucks was obvious within a couple of minutes of talking to him.  It will take a lot to get this store up and running at the level it was under his guidance and leadership.  Apologies if this seems like a soapbox stance, but I decided I should use this forum to voice my displeasure with the noticeably quick demise of the area's only truly great Starbucks location.  Good luck getting your s**t together again.

 he replies that jay is gone.    i don't go around offering reviews on personalities, etc., but the loss of jay as the manager of this store is gonna be crippling.  this man worked tirelessly to get to know every single customer, knew all of our drinks, always asked if everything was perfect, he was a walking coffee encyclopedia, was always sweeping the floors, making sure everything was just right.  i can go on, but you get the picture.  any of the regulars who knew jay will know exactly what i mean.  i ask a barista, "where's jay?"  strangely, the reply goes something like, "don't really know what happened.  i think the management culture makes the store manager's job impossible."  and i'm told several baristas have quit or asked to be reassigned as a result.  starbucks, i don't know what is going on here, and i don't know the entire story, but if you can't hold on to an employee the quality of jay, you have serious institutional issues.  i have never met a more caring, resourceful, hard working person as the man you have apparently now lost.  his dedication to starbucks was obvious within a couple of minutes of talking to him.  it will take a lot to get this store up and running at the level it was under his guidance and leadership.  apologies if this seems like a soapbox stance, but i decided i should use this forum to voice my displeasure with the noticeably quick demise of the area's only truly great starbucks location.  good luck getting your s**t together again.

THE LOWDOWN  They got most of the goods you want at a Starbucks, but they will make you pay for the "privilege" of being in an airport.

text: I am a huge Starbucks fan and was thrilled to see that this one was built right between my gym and my home! I went to get coffee the first day they were open and have been back several times since. I was disappointed on my first visit to discover that the employees were not as friendly as all the other Starbucks that I have been to. I decided to give them the benefit of the doubt since they had just opened and were possibly unfamiliar with the store. Unfortunately, this unfriendliness has not changed on any of my return visits. Until now, the quality was just as good as any Starbucks so I just overlooked the unfriendliness, but today something happened that forced me to write this review.   Today I ordered a latte Macchiato with an extra shot. I told the cashier my name, she wrote it on the cup, and I went to wait for my drink. Christin the barista, called my name when it was ready and announced my drink but it was not what I ordered. I politely asked, "what did you say"? She did not reply, and my partner said he couldn't hear her either. I leaned in and asked again politely "excuse me Miss, what did you say?" She looked at me and impatiently and said the name of the drink again which I still did not understand. I smiled and said "well that's my name but that's not what I ordered". To which she replied petulantly "well then I guess it's not yours". She then condescendingly proceeded to explain to me the difference between what I had ordered and what she had made. So I replied again politely "well you can understand my confusion since that is my name and there's no one else here waiting". She snapped "yes of course".   Nothing else was said, she went about her business, and then after about two minutes took the mistake drink and threw it away. In reality, she had made an error and was making me a new drink. Shortly after, she gave me the correct drink and said barked "here you go"  I'm not sure why, but for some reason the management of this Starbucks tolerates unfriendliness and bad service. This Starbucks is conveniently located for me so I will continue to go there, but I will recommend that you be ready to be treated poorly when you get your coffee.   June 24, 2016 Since the above review, I have returned to this Starbucks locations two or three times and I am happy to report that things have really changed! It seems like they have really gotten their act together, and the attitude is friendly and welcoming amongst the employees. Maybe it just took a little time for the new store employees to settle in, but whatever the reason, things are much better there now. I recommend this location as I would any other Starbucks location.

text: When my husband and I moved to the area two years ago, we were ecstatic to have a Starbucks with a drive-thru minutes from our house, but in the last six months or so, it's gone completely down hill - and the experience is the same inside and out.   First of all, there's a wait, 75-80% of the time.  They're just slow.  Unless you do a mobile order- so by all means, do a mobile order, but that does defeat the purpose of the drive-thru.  My lattes are always "light" -- meaning the cup isn't full (not even with foam) and there's a ton of foam in the portion that does have something in it.  And recently I've had 2 lattes that had an awful burnt aftertaste.  Once I even returned to have my drink re-made - I waited a good 20-30 minutes to be acknowledged and I feel like they were agitated about it.    Another time I received the completely wrong drink through the drive-thru, so we had to turn around and I had to go back in to get it fixed.    It's really frustrating - the location is nice (the patio is a perk) and the drive-thru is convenient, but if I can't get the correct drink and when I do I'm paying for 2/3 of a cup, what's the point?   Make the extra effort and go into the location in the Target- same parking lot.  Or the Giant on Henderson Road - their team there is AMAZING.  All I can say is this Starbucks needs to get back on their game.

text: This Starbuck's at the northeast corner of 6th and Olive Streets is the only downtown St. Louis Starbuck's that is not in a hotel. It is one of the four Starbucks in downtown St. Louis.    When I am on vacation, I like to explore and I like to relax. My dad and bro are the same way. It is in these Starbuck's that I got to enjoy quality time with my dad and bro. In all honesty, Starbuck's might not be the greatest coffee in the world. Personally I prefer Dunkin Donuts and Coffee Bean. Regardless their product is very good coffee. Along with their very good coffee, Starbuck's provides modern and comfortable hang out establishments that are known as the third-place. This was an ideal spot to sit with my dad and brother while sharing entertainment and sports news with our devices charging up in their outlets with complimentary Wi-Fi.    We visited this Starbucks twice in the mid morning. One visit was after the Embassy Suites Breakfast reception. I am not a fan of hotel coffee, and decided to wake up with Starbuck's in my cup. The other visit was after Pharoah Donuts. What is neat about Starbuck's near a donut shop is that the consumer benefits from a delicious pastry that is far better than the one in Starbuck's with a quality cup of coffee. If the donut shop is a hole in the wall with limited seating such as Pharoah Donuts, Starbuck's provides the customer with an enjoyable spot to delight in the donut(s).  Me, the consumer benefitted from this.   The tall (in Starbucks parlance small) coffee that was competively priced to other Starbucks at $2.33 tasted just as good as a Starbuck's coffee that I would sip in Las Vegas. The service was decent. And speaking of service, you need to ask the Barista the code for the restroom.     This Starbuck's has a downtown vibe. There were a sundry of downtown workers in and out of the building. It is a corner storefront at the base of a historic building. As I write this, construction cranes are very gradually transforming the immediate blocks around this downtown coffee shop from urban blight to a revitalizated neighborhood where historic buildings are being incorporated into homes. Along this stretch of Olive Street it seemed that the crane is more popular than the cardinal.     The two times that we started off our days here were prerequisites to downtown adventures. Rejuvenated with very good coffee, conversation, and in my case during one visit two very good donuts; we'd walk the city streets and parks to the western preiphery of downtown at St. Louis Union Station.

text: I am a huge Starbucks fan and was thrilled to see that this one was built right between my gym and my home! I went to get coffee the first day they were open and have been back several times since. I was disappointed on my first visit to discover that the employees were not as friendly as all the other Starbucks that I have been to. I decided to give them the benefit of the doubt since they had just opened and were possibly unfamiliar with the store. Unfortunately, this unfriendliness has not changed on any of my return visits. Until now, the quality of the coffee was just as good as any Starbucks so I just overlooked the unfriendliness, but today something happened that forced me to write this review.   Today I ordered a latte Macchiato with an extra shot. I told the cashier my name, she wrote it on the cup, and I went to wait for my drink. Christin the barista, called my name when it was ready and announced my drink but it was not what I ordered. I politely asked, "what did you say"? She did not reply, and my partner said he couldn't hear her either. I leaned in and asked again politely "excuse me Miss, what did you say?" She looked at me and impatiently and said the name of the drink again which I still did not understand. I smiled and said "well that's my name but that's not what I ordered". To which she replied petulantly "well then I guess it's not yours". She then condescendingly proceeded to explain to me the difference between what I had ordered and what she had made. So I replied again politely "well you can understand my confusion since that is my name and there's no one else here waiting". She snapped "yes of course".   Nothing else was said, she went about her business, and then after about two minutes took the mistake drink and threw it away. In reality, she had made an error and was making me a new drink. Shortly after, she gave me the correct drink and said barked "here you go"  I'm not sure why, but for some reason the management of this Starbucks tolerates unfriendliness and bad service. This Starbucks is conveniently located for me so I will continue to go there, but I will recommend that you be ready to be treated poorly when you get your coffee.

text: I have been a loyal customer of this Starbucks, mainly because of it's proximity to where I live, and while I am near a few Starbucks in the area, it seems that the baristas here are friendly and helpful, which is definitely a nice feature of a smaller Starbucks.  One of them has had very good suggestions on things, for example, today I tried a shot of Blonde Espresso in my Blonde Roast.  I never would have thought to have tried that but I actually really enjoyed that!  I was worried that espresso would have been way too strong and made me feel jittery but this was really good, except that it was a little strong on my stomach, but it definitely gave me the perk that I needed versus the plain Blonde roast.  She also had good suggestions when picking out a gift card for someone and I finally found the perfect one, which was really cute and said "Hey Thanks" as a cool thank you gift!  I felt that she went out of her way on a couple of occasions to help.  Now that this coffee shop is under new management, I have seen a lot of improvements in that I feel that the manager and people here really care and want you to be a happy customer, which I didn't feel before.  They've let me sample multiple coffees which were delicious.  My only reason for giving 4 stars instead of 5 is that I think sometimes there is confusion about the transactions of reloading my Gold card and then taking my balance out of the reload and sometimes the line moves a bit slowly.  All in all, I am happy here and I will continue to return, very nice neighborhood coffee shop!

text: I was HOPING that I would finally get my drink, ordered at "kids temp" (125 deg, warm)... actually at kids temp and NOT scalding hot. Wrong again. By the time I got my newly made drink, I was in a bit of a hurry, so I quickly grabbed my drink around the sleeve, dropped it in the cup holder and drove off. I looked at the cup where the printed sticker usually is (that shows what was ordered). Because they remade it, there was no sticker. Handwritten with a sharpie (in HUGE print) it read "X-HOT". Hmm. Disgruntled employee upset that I called them out for making my drink wrong? Were they trying to punish me for asking them to do their JOB correctly? I know what I thought, but you decide. I'd give anything for a half-priced-just-as-good mom and pop place nearby. I hate that this Starbucks is the only thing near me.

text: If you go when no one else is here, it is pretty on point. But below I will outline several issues that you should not have to face:  1) Terrible lines and poorly managed lines. I get it, this is on campus, so there are bound to be huge lines of students wanting coffee. During a lot of points one register is open but they will open up the second one to accommodate the line. But instead of making things better, this just means more people are standing in a crowded corner waiting for food. They also don't have a great system to get more people making drinks. I've been here several times when there are 40 people (not kidding) standing waiting for a drink, there is one person on a register, one making hot drinks, one making cold drinks, and there are then just two other baristas hanging around doing nothing. Just chatting.   2) There is Tap-N-Go confusion. The UA campus uses this mobile ordering service so imagine a line out the door, which is enough to deal with. Well then there are 40 other people ordering from the service. So now you have 80 people waiting for drinks, and 2 people making drinks.   3) Constant confusion on orders. This place is independently owned so they don't follow all of the normal rules/procedures/prices. Sometimes this leads to confusion. A drink I may order off-campus is sometimes up to 1$ cheaper. Half the time they want to charge you for ice. I once was charged TO TAKE ICE OUT OF A TEA! I said "no ice in the iced tea though" because I did not want it to be too cold, and the register actually charged me 50 cents. REALLY?! So there is some confusion in terms of pricing... but there is sometimes drink flavor issues too. Just depends on who is working.   4) They rush. They rush a lot (there are 80 drinks to be made!) and end up making simple mistakes. Like a drink will say no whipped cream, they put it on. I usually just scrape it off (dairy aversion) and don't mind, but these are little mistakes they should not be making.   5) Today I arrived at 8:58am and was in line. I was the 5th person in line and ordered at 9:02, which was pretty good. And then I did not get my latte until 9:33. Here are some of the reasons why:        (a) One of the baristas making drinks was working on two hot beverages when I was ordering and then, all of the sudden, she stops working and comes around the counter with the two drinks and gives them to two of her male friends that have just walked in. No mobile order info on the drinks. They did not pay. She proceeds to spend 8+ minutes talking to them while the line/drinks keep backing up. HUGE issue. When there are paying customers in line and a flood of people, employees should not be taking time to give free drinks to friends, and spending time chatting.
